the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition and set the multiplicity to zero.</li><li data-uuid="55917ace-71f1-4b6d-a198-f43153652f09"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e8e66d6-7698-45ad-98f5-c6739db3895f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the sysMLTableMenu command category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e30ba43-c8da-497e-9daf-45a436f217ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command category attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2725aea4-a54a-4e36-9039-d8ab57ed21d1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f39f2d1d-3b98-49cf-bc24-19055cfd44b0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3151637d-9b29-4ffe-ad9d-300e647f5daf">default</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the Symbolic Views category name (via <em>sysMLViewsMenu</em>)<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="c4c53b11-dda8-4045-bc57-99cbf6babb56"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Tabular Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the Tabular Views category name (via <em>sysMLTableMenu</em>)</span></li><li data-uuid="ea7dc03a-c3ab-4a86-8967-18d9bd0167ca"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span>// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f372b4-2387-4654-8e9e-76748d2ef534"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute is ignored.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c1a3070f-5c6b-4aa3-a030-defdf6a6ef6a">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition.</li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining category, </span>redefine <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span></li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d85b0fb-cb16-4ef0-8d6a-b5ead1b9832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// modified category's default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#AE2E24);">&quot;SysML v2 Symbolic Views&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// naming the symbolic views' creation command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// modified category's default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#AE2E24);">&quot;SysML v2 Tabular Views&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// naming the tabular views' creation command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465518da-8e26-400a-b5b8-7fd47b90ee9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Commands in categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5e50ad2a-7b75-49d7-87e7-a3e3ee5b4270"><em>sysMLViewsMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gvItem - </em>command for General View</li><li><em>ivItem - </em>command for Interconnection View</li><li><em>afvItem - </em>command for Action Flow View</li><li><em>stvItem - </em>command for State Transition View</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="92850224-2ef2-4e11-9dd0-f5773caba018"><em>sysMLTableMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gtItem </em>-<em> </em>command for General Table</li><li><em>rtItem</em> - command for Requirements Table</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To remove view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="d4a14b66-8231-41cb-b0fc-b49767269475"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category<em>.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="9b0b04eb-1607-45d9-b0d8-c2f7ab6e65dd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.</span></li><li data-uuid="345dca75-a7b2-46a0-aa5c-483afdd5a55a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48143ee6-7510-4b2c-acf2-ca062bd903c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the afvItem command from the sysMLViewsMenu category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Renaming view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To rename view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="32b106af-1ebb-4823-85f4-89d12d6c6f99"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.</span></li><li data-uuid="6c874d68-3f66-48d5-958a-e93fd4f1eba0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited <em>label </em>attribute with the needed value.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="be761cfb-aa2f-4020-a7d8-24c381848ffc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="657cc2ff-7dc8-4a04-8146-1f8a6bb0c486"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Action Flow Diagram&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified command name </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="e10258c1-074d-4e2f-82cf-c8c0939729fc">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating View Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005430 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: (2026x Refresh1) Dassault Systemes Functions

- page_id: `304005430`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005430/2026x+Refresh1+Dassault+Systemes+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:31.288+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists the functions available in the *DassaultSystemesFunctions* namespace of the 3DS SysML Customization library.

| Function | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| calc def IsEssentialElement { in input : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "elementFilter" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.EssentialElements::isEssentialInOwnershipContext*/} | filter DS_Functions::IsEssentialElement(self);Click here to see the full example...package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs; part vehicleRecord;}view 'Vehicle DetailsView' : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { expose 'Vehicle Information'::**; filter DS_Functions::IsEssentialElement(self); } | This function checks whether the <input> element is treated as an essential element.Can be used only in a . |
| calc def IsNonStandardLibraryElement { in input : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "elementFilter" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.libraries.StandardLibraries::isNotFromLibrary*/} | filter DS_Functions::IsNonStandardLibraryElement(self);Click here to see the full example...package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs; part vehicleRecord;}view 'Vehicle DetailsView' : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { expose 'Vehicle Information'::**; filter DS_Functions::IsNonStandardLibraryElement(self);} | This function checks whether the <input> element comes from a non-standard library.Can be used only in a . |
| calc def IsSpecific { in specific : Element; in general : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.isSpecific */} | part def ModelLibraryElement :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part electrical :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { IsSpecific(element, ElectricalPart.metadata) } } part :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> penColor default "#0078FF"; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { part def ElectricalPart; part def MechanicalPart; part electrical : ElectricalPart; part mechanical : MechanicalPart;}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { part componentA :> electrical; part componentB :> mechanical; } view userModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : ModelLibraryElement :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def ModelLibraryElement :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part electrical :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { DS_Functions::IsSpecific(element, 'Concepts & Keywords'::ElectricalPart.metadata) } } part :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> penColor default "#0078FF"; } }} | This function checks whether the <specific> feature is directly or indirectly specialized by the <general> feature. |
| calc def GetAppliedMetadata { in element : Element; return [0..*] : Metadata::MetadataItem; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getAppliedMetadata */} | part def 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { (GetAppliedMetadata(element) as ComponentImportance).isImportant == true } } view :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> fillColor = "#CCAAAA"; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { metadata def ComponentImportance { :>> annotatedElement : SysML::Systems::PartUsage; attribute isImportant : ScalarValues::Boolean; }}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { part componentA { @ComponentImportance { :>> isImportant = true; } } part componentB { @ComponentImportance { :>> isImportant = false; } } } view userModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { (DS_Functions::GetAppliedMetadata(element) as 'Concepts & Keywords'::ComponentImportance).isImportant == true } } view :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> fillColor = "#CCAAAA"; } }} | This function returns all metadata usages that annotate the <input> element. |
| calc def GetSpecifiedBy { in specific [0..*] : Element; in general : Element; return [0..*] : Type = specific.? { in x; IsSpecific(x, general) };} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; expose UserModel::vehicle::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Color Attributes' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : ElementCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = GetSpecifiedBy((rowElement as SysML::Element).ownedElement, Color.metadata); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { attribute def Color; attribute def Mass;}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part vehicle { attribute color : Color; attribute mass : Mass; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::vehicle::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Color Attributes' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::ElementCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::GetSpecifiedBy((rowElement as SysML::Element).ownedElement, 'Concepts & Keywords'::Color.metadata); } } } }} | This function returns the <specific> feature if it is directly or indirectly specialized by the <general> feature. |
| calc def GetValue { in contextType : Element; in feature : Feature; return [0..*] : Base::Anything; language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getValue */} | part def 'Electrical::isPowered' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { GetValue(element, isPowered.metadata) == true } } part :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> lineWidth default 3; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { part def ElectricalPart; part electrical : ElectricalPart { attribute isPowered : ScalarValues::Boolean; } metadata def Electrical :> Metaobjects::SemanticMetadata { :>> baseType = electrical meta SysML::Systems::Usage; :>> annotatedElement : SysML::Systems::PartUsage; }}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { #Electrical part componentA { :>> isPowered = true; } #Electrical part componentB { :>> isPowered = false; } } view UserModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : 'Electrical::isPowered' :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def 'Electrical::isPowered' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { DS_Functions::GetValue(element, 'Concepts & Keywords'::electrical::isPowered.metadata) == true } } part :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> lineWidth default 3; } }} | This function returns the values of all features in the <contextType> that match or redefine the specified <feature>. |
| calc def GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency { in context : Element; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getSuppliersOfOwnedDependency */ return [0..*] : Element;} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Supplier of Owned Dependency' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : ElementCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency(rowElement); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package UserModel { part system { dependency from componentA to componentB; part componentA; part componentB; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Supplier of Owned Dependency' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::ElementCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency(rowElement); } } } }} | This function returns the list of suppliers from Dependency relations owned by the <context>, where <context> acts as the client of the dependency. |
| calc def IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency { in context : Element; in element : Element; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.isNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency */ return [1] : Boolean;} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Not A Supplier of Owned Dependency' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : BooleanCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency(rowElement, componentB.metadata); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package UserModel { part system { dependency from componentA to componentB; part componentA; part componentB; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Not A Supplier of Owned Dependency' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::BooleanCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency(rowElement, componentB.metadata); } } } }} | This function checks whether the <element> is not among the suppliers of Dependency relations owned by the <context>, where <context> acts as the client of the dependency. |

}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="2536b132-fb83-43b9-a7f6-342c37724fec">This function checks whether the &lt;element&gt; is not among the suppliers of Dependency relations owned by the &lt;context&gt;, where &lt;context&gt; acts as the client of the dependency.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006467 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: (2026x Refresh1) DassaultSystemesValidationSuite

- page_id: `304006467`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006467/2026x+Refresh1+DassaultSystemesValidationSuite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:30.679+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation > (2026x Refresh1) Predefined validation suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists all the validation rules in the *DassaultSystemesValidationSuite*, along with their messages and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results' space=''].

References such as *$notVariantUsages* are used in messages to indicate elements referenced by the validation rule. In the tool, they are replaced with hyperlinks to the actual elements requiring your attention to resolve the validation failure.

| Validation rule | Validation rule message | Severity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| validateEnumType | Must be typed by exactly one enumeration definition. Currently typed by: $types. | Error |
| Must be typed by an enumeration definition. |  |  |
| validateUsageTypedByClassifier | Must be typed by a classifier. Currently typed by $featureTypingType. | Error |
| validateFeatureTypeConformance | Feature type must conform to the type of its value's result. | Warning |
| validateShapeOwner | Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership in the model.ConditionApplies to all OwningMemberships, except FeatureMembership | Warning |
| Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership or inheritance in the model.ConditionApplies to all FeatureMemberships | Warning |  |
| validatePathOverlapping | A path must not overlap another path. | Warning |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Currently typed by $featureTypingType.</td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureTypeConformance</p></td><td>Feature type must conform to the type of its value's result.</td><td>Warning</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validateShapeOwner</p><br /><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership in the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aca8b894-b7d5-42c1-baed-92a30129ac26"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Applies to all OwningMemberships, except FeatureMembership</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Warning</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership or inheritance in the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="919ececb-9e3a-4f79-908a-57e06c81f40a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Applies to all FeatureMemberships</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Warning</td></tr><tr><td><p>validatePathOverlapping</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A path must not overlap another path.</p></div></td><td>Warning</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005798 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: (2026x Refresh1) Dependency

- page_id: `304005798`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005798/2026x+Refresh1+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:46.502+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations > (2026x Refresh1) Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A dependency is a kind of relationship between any number of client (source) and supplier (target) elements. This implies that a change to a supplier element may result in a change to a client element.Dependencies can be useful for representing relationships between elements in an abstract way.Dependencies can be binary, with one client and supplier, or n-ary, with one client and more than one supplier.The n-ary dependency is displayed as a dot symbol with segments consisting of dependency paths and client/supplier symbols.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *Use*dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source) and*Data Layer*is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source). The packages*Data Layer*and*External Interface Layer*are the supplier (target).The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

Dependency is deleted if client or supplier count in the dependency is reduced zero.

##### Creating dependencies

###### Creating dependencies via the Textual Editor

To create dependencies via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the dependency keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the client (source) element. Then follow with the keyword to and the name(s) of the supplier (target) element(s) listed in a comma-seperated list. If the dependency has no name, the keyword*from*can be omitted.
3. (Optional) Specify a body with any annotating elements owned by the dependency via annotation relationships.
4. Click the Synchronize button. package'Data Layer';package'Service Layer';package'External Interface Layer';dependencyfrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // unnamed dependencydependencyUsefrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // named dependencydependency from 'Service Layer' to 'External Interface Layer', 'Data Layer'; // unnamed n-ary dependency (with more than one supplier (target))dependency'Service Layer'to'Data Layer'{ // unnamed dependency with a body 
/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */ 
}

###### Creating dependencies via the view palette

To create dependencies via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **dependency** button.
2. In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.

###### Creating dependenciesvia the Create Relation command

To create dependencies via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click dependency . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating n-ary dependencies via the view

To create n-ary dependencies via the view

1. .
2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the client command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.
  2. Click the supplier command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary dependencies

###### Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view

To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-arydependencytobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier.

Loading rewrite

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006396 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: (2026x Refresh1) Derive requirement

- page_id: `304006396`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006396/2026x+Refresh1+Derive+requirement
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.928+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Derived requirements are created using the derive relationship via the derivation connection. The derive relationship links more detailed requirements to their original, higher-level requirements. A derivation connection asserts that one or more derived requirements are derived from a single original requirement. The single end for the original requirement should subset the original requirement, while the rest of the ends should subset derived requirements. Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.

##### Deriving requirements

###### Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor

To derive requirements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the RequirementDerivation library.
2. Declare the #derivation conncetion keyword, specify the derivation name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] (optional).
3. In the derivation body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in separate packages

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling; 
} 
packageDerivations{// derivations package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
privateimportRequirements::*;// requirements package import 
#derivationconnection : 'Derived System Requirements'{// unnamed specialized (feature typing) binary derivation connection 
end#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement end 
end#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement end 
} 
}

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in the same package

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling;#derivationconnection'heating & cooling derivations'{// named n-ary derivation connectionend#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement endend#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement endend#derive::>'cooling';// derived requirement end}}

###### Deriving requirements via the view palette

To derive requirements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:
  1. The **derivation** button to create a derivation connection.
    1. In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.
  2. The **n-ary****derivation** button to create an n-ary derivation connection.
    1. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.
    2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **original** end command.
    3. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.
    4. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **derived** end command.
    5. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation**,**n-ary derivation**,****or the**derivation****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view

To create additional n-ary derivations via the view

1. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the original end command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement.
  2. Click the **derived end** command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary derivations

###### Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view

To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryderivationtobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier.

##### Creating derivation connection definition elements

###### Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the #derivation connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] of the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] of the derived requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. #derivationconnectiondef'Derived system requirements'{ 
end#originaloriginalReq:vehicleRequirements; 
end#derivederivedReq:engineRequirements; 
}

###### Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating derivation definitions via the view palette

To create derivation definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **derivation** **def** button to create a derivation definition element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation** or**n-ary derivation**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Derived requirements are created using the derive relationship via the derivation connection. The derive relationship links more detailed requirements to their original, higher-level requirements. A derivation connection asserts that one or more derived requirements are derived from a single original requirement. The single end for the original requirement should subset the original requirement, while the rest of the ends should subset derived requirements. Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0564479-1546-4b81-bfe5-e2ee43d3395b"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="faa43dee-fd89-4e11-98d5-91d76c3846e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="026e7ceb-bd4b-47fc-8978-293f8804db7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c45965e4-c2f0-4147-8f06-8524b0e6feaa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Deriving requirements</h3><h4>Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To derive requirements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the <em>RequirementDerivation </em>library.</li><li>Declare the <em>#derivation conncetion</em> keyword, specify the derivation name (optional),<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(optional).</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the derivation body:</span><ol><li>Specify the <em>end #original </em>keyword <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original requirement. </li><li>Specify the <em>end #derive </em>keyword <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>derivation</strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>def </strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">button to create a derivation definition element.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35819f6b-9a74-40cd-bfc8-eac27c667eb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>derivation</strong> or<strong> n-ary derivation </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006240 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: (2026x Refresh1) Directed features for actions

- page_id: `304006240`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006240/2026x+Refresh1+Directed+features+for+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:14.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actions may have features with directions in, out or inout that are considered to be parameters of the action. Features with direction in or inout are input parameters, and features with direction out or inout are output parameters. An action usage inherits the parameters of its definitions, if any, and it can also define its own parameters or redefine those of its definitions. Directed features may also be bound to indicate the equivalence of their values.

##### Specifying directed features for actions

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the action's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name. actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; // *in* directed feature 
outpicture:Picture; // *out* directed feature}

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the view palette

To specify directed features for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the action for which you want to create the element.

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an action symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

##### Binding directed features

Directed features can also bebound. An output parameter of one action usage may be bound to the input parameter of another action usage. Such a binding indicates that the values of the target input parameter will always be the same as the values of the source output parameter. For more information on binding, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Binding' space=''] page.

actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; 
outpicture:Picture;

// binding *in*parameters TakePicture.scene with focus.scene 
bindscene=focus.scene; 
 
actionfocus:Focus{inscene;outimage; } 
firstfocusthenshoot; 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
actionshoot:Shoot{inimage;outpicture; } 
 
// binding *out*parameters TakePicture.picture with shoot.picture 
bindpicture=shoot.picture; 
}

The binding of action parameters does not model the case when there is an actual transfer of items between actions. Transfers can be modeled using flows, succession flows as well as send and accept actions. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Transfers between actions' space=''] page.

##### Redefining parameters

Each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:

50%

actiondefImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId; 
incustomerName; 
incustomerEmail; 
} 
actionimportCustomerData:ImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId 
incustomerName;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName 
incustomerEmail;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail 
incustomerPhoneNumber;// new owned parameter 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Redefinition' space='']

The redefinitions are created as follows:

- If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 2.png']
- The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters customerId and validationId are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 3.png']
- The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters <em>customerId </em>and <em>validationId </em>are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Directed features for actions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="ddd4d155-cb21-4413-9a36-a6fda316b0c6">The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.</li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005961 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2026x Refresh1) Directed features for states

- page_id: `304005961`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005961/2026x+Refresh1+Directed+features+for+states
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:53.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specify features with directions *in*, *out,*or *inout*for states that act as the parameters of the state. Directed features can also be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Binding' space=''].

##### Specifying directed features for states

###### Specifying directed features for states via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for states via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the state's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name.

statedefVehicleStates{inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle;}

###### Specifying directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select a state symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

###### Specifying directed features for states via the view palette

To specify directed features for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the state for which you want to create the element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005989 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: (2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views

- page_id: `304005989`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005989/2026x+Refresh1+Displaying+elements+in+symbolic+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:55.187+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Display commands to display element symbols in a graphical representation of a symbolic view.

##### Display commands

| Display command category and its commands | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Nested-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the nested-view . Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as nested symbols. |
| Display Features | The command displays owned and inherited features of any metatype.Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as element symbols. To display them as shapes on border, use the Display Parameters command. |
| Display Features Recursively | The command displays all owned and inherited features of any metatype recursively. |
| Display Parts | The command displays owned and inherited parts. |
| Display Parts Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited parts recursively. |
| Display Actions | The command displays owned and inherited actions. |
| Display Actions Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited actions recursively. |
| Display Swimlanes | The command displays owned and inherited swimlane symbols, and any element symbols related to each swimlane.The command is only available for specific and . |
| Display Owned Members | The command displays owned members based on the 'ownedMember' property's value.The command is only available for . |
| Display Owned Members Recursively | The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value.The command is only available for . |
| Tree-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view .Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.Contains all the same commands as the Nested-view style category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.The Display Features command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style. |
| Relations | The category contains commands for displaying relation paths of the selected symbol. |
| Display Relations | The command displays:paths of all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. Connector paths can also be .paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.paths of other relations (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.paths that exist between symbols of an element that owns or inherits features of the applicable model element (see below) and the referenced element connected via reference subsetting.Applicable elements: , , , , , , , , , frame concern usage, .Example... |
| Display Relations Excluding Specializations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, excluding paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.). |
| Display In-Between Relations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, but only between the selected element symbols.This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Display In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, but only between the selected element symbols, excluding paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Other | The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols. |
| Display Ports | The command displays owned and inherited ports.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Parameters | The command displays owned and inherited directional features.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as shapes on border. To display them as element symbols, use the Display Features command. |
| Display Ends | The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.The command is only available for . |
| Display Exposed Elements | The command displays elements exposed for the view. Exposed elements depend on the (e.g., recursivity) and . The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a .This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an . |

#### NOTE: Recursive commands

Recursive commands

Recursive commands are indicated by the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the *drone in SAR mission*part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.

There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the **Display**commands to be available (except for the **Display Exposed Elements**command).

##### Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)Displaying element symbols in a view

###### Displaying element symbols

To display element symbols

1. In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display. To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.Use the **Select**buttons in the **Select**option group in the view panel.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Display button [ATTACHMENT filename='display_button.png'] in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.
  2. Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select Display . Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.

##### Displaying connectors in a view

In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol using the**Display Relations**command. See theprocedure.

###### Proxy connectors

Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via the feature chain.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partlugBoltJoints:LugBoltJoint[0..5]; 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
partmountingHoles:LugBoltMountingHole[5]; 
} 
} 
connect[0..1]lugBoltJointsto[1]wheel.w.mountingHoles; 
}

###### In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part *lugBoltJoints*and part *mountingHoles* nested within part *w*, which is nested within the part *wheel*. 
Since the nested part symbols*w*and*mountingHoles*are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between*lugBoltJoints*and*wheel*, which is a displayed parent element symbol of*w*and*mountingHoles.*To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part*wheel*and the connector path.A connector label with the*w.mountingHoles*feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to.

###### Elaborating a connector

The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.

To elaborate/simplify a connector

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Elaborate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is elaborated.
  2. To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Simplify [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Connector end labels

In a view, the connector path ends are displayed with labels containing the information regarding each connector end. The label may include:

- Metadata 
e.g. *«#CustomReqMetadata»*
- Name or proxy connector end '<'declared short name'> 'declared name' ::> 'proxy connection end'; e.g. <END_1.1> org ::> .vehicle mass
- Multiplicity 
e.g. *[1]*
- Metadata values 
e.g: *{author = "John"; assignee = "Victor";}*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying n-ary connectors in a view

In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.

The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) N-ary connection' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) N-ary interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Derive requirement' space='']

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Dependency' space=''] can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths between the selected elements and the n-ary dot symbol via the **Display Relations**command.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

###### Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view

To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view

- Select the containing element symbol and click the Display command, then in its dropdown menu, click Display Features . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view

To display n-ary connector ends in a view

- Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the Display command. In its dropdown menu, click Display Ends . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

- Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the Display command, then Display Relations . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png']

##### Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters

Hovering over a feature represented as a shape on border (e.g., ports, parameters), a tooltip is shown, displaying both owned and inherited attributes and items.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
