ac:macro-id="17a138b4-b209-49de-8fe4-cdef8abb6186"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_references.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Relations Excluding Specializations</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, excluding </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display In-Between Relations</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, but only between the selected element symbols</span>.</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="47e56910-677d-413e-9ef7-e0a7212f104c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>   Display In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations</td><td><p><span>The command displays <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, but only between the selected element symbols, </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">excluding </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="e5bfb3dd-44b1-419c-a46c-1ee8b96f5497"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title="">Other</strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ports</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited ports.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea0381f5-1e71-42b5-8879-4601ea095343"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parameters </td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited directional features.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71db2b8d-7fa0-4306-8ff7-8971ca47c54b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="c0433290-6aaa-4b4e-9a3e-897df77bb988"><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></li><li data-uuid="63307655-e75f-4177-a062-15c5c6dafb1b"><span>Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as shapes on border. To display them as element symbols, use the <strong>Display Features</strong> command.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ends</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18987458-ba47-4c34-847d-ee44388c3570"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command is only available for <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying n-ary connectors in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[n-ary dot symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span>   Display Exposed Elements</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays elements exposed for the view. <span>Exposed elements depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposition kind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., recursivity) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7866e435-7592-4369-ade6-4bcb219fed2e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4acb477e-c9ee-4f2b-9122-83b716c28268"><span>The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="18dd3bff-bbf5-408d-a8d2-3c366eeee6eb"><p>This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display exposed elements notification.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9bdb1c-48d3-4406-b1a5-fc676442b857"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Recursive commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Recursive commands are indicated by the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_button.png" /></ac:image> recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_cmd.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_commands.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the <em>drone in SAR mission </em>part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722af941-19b0-4426-a909-ef6eb0ae1b9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the <strong>Display </strong>commands to be available (except for the <strong>Display Exposed Elements </strong>command).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd72916b-65f7-44c1-ba2b-3b9ed1c54c30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying element symbols in a view</h3><h4>Displaying element symbols</h4><p>To display element symbols</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c2dfbd-2cfc-428a-b545-87f6715e5c90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f41dad1-5ad0-45d3-a18e-743b57ba085f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.</li><li>Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol. </li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.</li><li>Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.</li><li>Use the <strong>Select </strong>buttons in the <strong>Select </strong>option group in the view panel.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></p><ol><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_button.png" /></ac:image> in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong>. Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Displaying connectors in a view</h3><p>In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Relations </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command. S</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ee the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying element symbols" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> procedure.</span></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Proxy connectors</span></h4><p>Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via the feature chain.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a5bc18e-4ea8-44b6-b1ad-8d1b0b980e90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28baaa67-c188-4eed-811b-ebb046e222bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Proxy_connectors.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1776f58-016f-4488-b406-dca75f670789"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="544bb35c-59cb-48bf-978e-4d57ff4349f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltJoint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltMountingHole</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">]</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part <em>lugBoltJoints </em>and part <em>mountingHoles</em> nested within part <em>w</em>, which is nested within the part <em>wheel</em>.<br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Since the nested part symbols </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">lugBoltJoints </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, which is a displayed parent element symbol of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles.<br /></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and the connector path.<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A connector label with the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w.mountingHoles</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to. </span></h6><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Elaborating a connector</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.</span></p><p>To elaborate/simplify a connector</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Elaborate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elaborate_button.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>command. The connector is elaborated.</li><li>To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Simplify <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simplify_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.</li></ol></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Elaborating_a_connector.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Connector end labels</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>In a view, the connector path ends are displayed with labels containing the information regarding each connector end. The label may include:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="9533f342-06f8-4af0-ba19-3e010d6f4e30"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Metadata<br />e.g. <em>«#CustomReqMetadata»</em><br /></span></span></li><li data-uuid="a7f4b690-e159-4497-8d2f-6584a9d32955"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Name or proxy connector end<br /></span></span>'&lt;'declared short name'&gt; 'declared name' ::&gt; 'proxy connection end'; e.g. <em>&lt;END_1.1&gt; org ::&gt; .vehicle mass</em></li><li data-uuid="482d9ce8-c81e-42ae-8b5f-6d189345d0fa"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Multiplicity<br />e.g. <em>[1]</em><br /></span></span></li><li data-uuid="95037af4-d39b-400f-bfb6-8ac87bf4eb11"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Metadata values <br />e.g: <em>{author = &quot;John&quot;; assignee = &quot;Victor&quot;;}</em> </span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector_end_labels.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Displaying n-ary connectors in a view</h3><p>In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.</span></p><p>The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) N-ary connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) N-ary interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Derive requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derivation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f43002-a869-49a2-b0fa-21b855d3bb07"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths between the selected elements and the n-ary dot symbol via the <strong>Display Relations </strong>command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bcd8a03-8277-409e-8ebf-4ac42d0493c0"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a4d778b-bd2c-46b8-a56a-5b09282ce5c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c339a57-b3ef-4d56-8b0d-79d86eb7fc5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the containing element symbol and click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Features</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connector ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the <strong>Display</strong> command. In its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Ends</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then <strong>Display Relations</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters </h3><p>Hovering over a <span>feature represented as a shape on border (e.g., ports, parameters), a tooltip is shown, displaying both owned and inherited attributes and items.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters .png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005983 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00077: (2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in tabular views

- page_id: `304005983`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005983/2026x+Refresh1+Displaying+elements+in+tabular+views
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:19:02.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.

Tabular views display up to 50,000 rows. If building a table exceeds this limit, the tool displays a notification suggesting that you narrow the table scope or adjust the display mode settings and try again.

##### Displaying elements in tabular views

To display elements in tabular views

1. Ensure the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating tabular views' space=''] view has:
  1. Tabular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rendering views' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
  3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Opening views' space=''] the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.
3. (Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_table_button.png'] Refresh button in the view toolbar to update the view.

##### Managing table elements display mode

The table elements' display mode allows you to specify how the exposed elements should be displayed in the table. The default display mode for all tables is **List**. The display mode can be managed via the Textual Editor using dedicated attributes or the table toolbar's **Options** command. See the table below, indicating the display mode options available via the table toolbar's **Options** command and the corresponding attributes used in the textual notation.

| Display Mode (Options command) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Complete Tree | Shows each element with all of its owners (direct, indirect, and common).Groups elements under common owners, including those higher up in the ownership hierarchy. |
| Compact Tree | Shows each element with its direct owners.Groups elements by their common owners when multiple elements share the same one. The omitted indirect owners are part of the common owner path's qualified name, e.g., indirectOwner::commonOwner. Omits indirect owners higher in the hierarchy. |
| List | Displays the exposed elements as a plain list.List is the default display mode for all tables. |

#### NOTE: Ownership explanation

Ownership explanation

- Direct owner: the immediate owner of an element.
- Indirect owner: an owner higher in the hierarchy (not immediate).
- Common owner: the same element that owns multiple exposed elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing table elements display mode via the Options command

To manage table elements' display mode via the Options command

- In the tabular view's toolbar, click the Options[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button, and in the dropdown menu, click the needed display mode option: **Complete Tree**, **Compact Tree**, **List**. The table elements are displayed in the selected display mode.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005560 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2026x Refresh1) Docked symbols

- page_id: `304005560`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005560/2026x+Refresh1+Docked+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:37.168+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with views > (2026x Refresh1) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2, an element symbol can be docked to a view, acting as a context and a kind of frame for a view.

**Docked symbol details:**

- The element represented as the docked symbol is the owner of elements whose symbols are contained by the docked symbol.
- Element symbols cannot be created outside the docked symbols' shape in the view pane. Creating element symbols outside the docked symbol's frame expands the frame to contain the new symbols.
- A view can have only one docked symbol.
- The docked element symbol can be undocked and vice versa. See the procedures below.
- Executing the 'Select in Containment tree' command:
  - on the docked symbol's body - selects the corresponding view.
  - on the docked symbol's header - selects the corresponding element .

##### Docking a symbol to a view

To dock a symbol to a view

- To create a view with a docked symbol:
  - In the Containment tree, right-click a usage or a definition element and in the shortcut menu, click **Create view**.
- To dock a symbol displayed in a view:
  - Select the element symbol in the view and click the **Dock** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button on the corner of the element frame. The **Dock**button is available only if the view displays one element symbol (e.g., the docking option is not available if there are two part usage symbols displayed in the view).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Docking a symbol to a view

##### Undocking a symbol from a view

To undock a symbol from a view

- Right-click the header of the docked symbol and in the shortcut menu, select **Refactor**, then **Undock**. Right-clicking anywhere but the header of the docked symbol will not display the **Undock**command in the shortcut menu.Once you undock a symbol, it remains the same size, taking up the entire view pane. Click the **Make Preferred Size** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Undocking a symbol from a view

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</strong>command in the shortcut menu.</li><li>Once you undock a symbol, it remains the same size, taking up the entire view pane. Click the <strong>Make Preferred Size</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Make_Preferred_Size_button.png" /></ac:image> button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Undocking_a_symbol_from_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image>Undocking a symbol from a view</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005688 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler

- page_id: `304005688`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005688/2026x+Refresh1+Drag+and+Drop+handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:43.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands.

##### Using the Drag and Drop handler

To use the Drag and Drop handler

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.
  2. In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the Drag and Drop handler [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop_handler.png'] . Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change' space='']. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol.
2. Drag and drop the element/symbol on:
  1. The target element symbol in a view.
  2. The appropriate field in the Specification panel. You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change' space=''].You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.
3. The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.

##### Drag and Drop handler commands

Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols.

| Command name | Source | Target |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set Type>]]>If an element already has a set type, performing the Set Type command again replaces the set type. To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. | Any definition element | Any usage element |
| Set Trigger>]]> | Any definition element | Transition |
| Set Payload>]]> | Any definition element | Accept Action |
| Create Flow>]]> | Item def, Attribute def, Part def | Connection |
| Set Performed Action>]]> | Action | Perform Action |
| Set Use Case Included>]]> | Use Case | Include Use Case |
| Set Exhibited State>]]> | State | Exhibit State |
| Set Required Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Require Constraint |
| Set Assumed Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Assume Constraint |
| Set Asserted Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Assert Constraint |
| Set Satisfied Requirement>]]> | Requirement | Satisfy Requirement |
| Set Verified Requirement>]]> | Requirement | Requirement Usage Owned via Requirement Verification Membership |
| Set Referenced Rendering>]]> | Rendering | Rendering |
| Set Framed Concern>]]> | Concern | Frame Concern |
| Set Event Occurrence>]]> | Occurrence | Event Occurrence |
| Create Perform Action>]]> | Action, Action def | Part, Action, State, Part def, Action def, State def |
| Create Exhibit State>]]> | State, State def | Part, State, Action, Part def, State def, Action def |
| Create Include Use Case>]]> | Use Case, Use Case def | Part, Use Case, Part def, Use Case def |
| Create ObjectiveLearn more >]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Case, Case def |
| Create Require Constraint of ObjectiveLearn more >]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Case, Case def |
| Create Verification Requirement of Objective>]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Case, Case def |
| Create ActorLearn more >]]> and >]]> | Part, Part def | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Create StakeholderLearn more >]]> | Part, Part def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Frame Concern>]]> | Concern, Concern def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Require Constraint>]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assume Constraint>]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assert Constraint>]]> | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Satisfy Requirement>]]> | Requirement, Requirement def | Part |
| Create SubjectLearn more >]]> and >]]> | Any usage element,Any definition element | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Apply Metadata>]]> | Metadata def | Any element |
| Create Usage | Any definition element | Any element |
| Create Subsetting Usage>]]> | Any usage element | Any element |
| Set Subsetted Feature>]]> | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Redefined Feature>]]> | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Value | Any feature | Any feature |
| Set Superclassifier>]]> | Any definition element | Any definition element |
| Create Symbol | Any element | Any element |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands. </p><h3>Using the Drag and Drop handler</h3><p>To use the Drag and Drop handler</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.</li><li>In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the <strong>Drag and Drop</strong> handler <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop_handler.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ad23bda-3eee-4c7a-a876-604aa6d7d064"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[changes the element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol<span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag and drop the element/symbol on:</p><ol><li data-uuid="34ef9f28-0bfc-47b5-9804-1813127c01f9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The target element symbol in a view.</p></li><li data-uuid="52c8143b-14dc-4082-84c1-0bda4045e065"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The appropriate field in the Specification panel.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a919802e-8de9-48f5-a232-551e6eb9441a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.</li><li data-uuid="4d4bfd40-e75f-454d-8294-154efa4aecde">For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying additional properties via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.</li></ol><h3>Drag and Drop handler commands</h3><p>Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command name</th><th scope="col">Source</th><th scope="col">Target</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set Type</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Defining an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36da9b09-77c1-4a65-9384-34eb3be67cc8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an element already has a set type, performing the <strong>Set Type</strong> command again replaces the set type. <br />To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Any usage element</td></tr><tr><td><p>Set Trigger</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying triggers for transitions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Transition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Transition</td></tr><tr><td><p>Set Payload</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying the payload for accept actions via <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005715 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: (2026x Refresh1) Editor Tabs

- page_id: `304005715`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005715/2026x+Refresh1+Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:44.299+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling tool environment > (2026x Refresh1) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- View Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.
- Textual Editor tab. It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.
- Specification tab. It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.</p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c54f6dbc-5c61-4b6c-a314-2ad6f152913a">Editor tab types</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:</p><ul><li>Welcome tab.<br />The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.</li><li>Samples tab.<br />It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.</li><li>View Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.</li><li>Textual Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.</li><li data-uuid="975212db-3869-4ac2-9eec-3ae21f774437">Specification tab.<br />It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.  </li></ul><h3>Editor tabs commands</h3><p>Editor tabs' commands can be found:</p><ul><li>In the <strong>Main Menu</strong>, click <strong>Window</strong>, then <strong>Editor Tabs</strong>. In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006217 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x Refresh1) Element names

- page_id: `304006217`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006217/2026x+Refresh1+Element+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:12.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the information on this page for naming elements.

- For information on name resolution (i.e., feature chaining and qualified names), see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] page.
- Use Short Name Instead of Name and Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement environment options allow you to control whether a declared short name or declared name should be used for elements in the textual notation. Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] page.

- Declared name
  - In addition to the keyword indicating element kind, the declaration of an element may also specify a declared name. The declared name should be fully descriptive, particularly in the context of the element definition. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] page. part'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 partbulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

- Declared short name
  - The declared short name, if given, should be an abbreviated name useful for referring to the element. The declared short name is distinguished by being surrounded by the delimiting characters < and >. The declared short name, if used, must precede the declared name. part <cyl> cylinders; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylindersrequirement<'1'>fullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement; // a requirement with the declared short name <'1'>, which is also considered its requirement ID.

- Name and short name
  - If neither a declared name or a declared short name are given in the declaration of a usage, then it is determined by the name or short name of the redefined usage. Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. part def Engine { 
 part <cyl> cylinders : Cylinder [2 ..*]; 
} 
part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine { 
 part redefines cylinders [4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its name is "cylinders". 
// OR 
 part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

##### Specifying the declared name

###### Specifying the declared name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword or declared short name with the needed basic or unrestricted name. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation' space=''] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part 'LED Flashlight'{ // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

###### Specifying the declared name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

###### Specifying the declared name via the Containment tree

To specify the declared name via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, double-click an element and type the needed name.

###### Specifying the declared name via the element symbol in a view

To specify the declared name via the element symbol in a view

- On a symbol on view, double-click the name line under the element keyword and type the needed name.

##### Specifying the declared short name

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared short name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword with the <> symbols with the needed short name within them.
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
part<cyl>cylinders:Cylinder[2..*]; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylinders 
}part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine {part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared short name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">See the information on this page for naming elements.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="635672fe-edeb-4f33-8e23-b8d8439be8ab"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For information on name resolution (i.e., feature chaining and qualified names), see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Name resolution]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing textual notation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li data-uuid="21854039-1c53-443c-8122-8c1c1c606a9a"><strong>Use Short Name Instead of Name </strong>and <strong>Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement </strong>environment options allow you to control whether a declared short name or declared name should be used for elements in the textual notation. Learn more on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Environment options" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li><strong>Declared name</strong><ul><li>In addition to the keyword indicating element kind, the declaration of an element may also specify a declared name. The declared name should be fully descriptive, particularly in the context of the element definition.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b528899d-bd43-4be0-9603-87b263922fdf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Naming"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Naming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Naming"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing textual notation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c144f8a-2742-42f3-91f5-471b3ca1daa2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">LED Flashlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span> </span> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unrestricted element name that includes whitespace</span> </span> <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bulb</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">//<span> </span> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">basic element name without whitespace</span> </span> <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Declared short name</strong><ul><li><span>The declared short name, if given, should be an abbreviated name useful for referring to the element. The declared short name is distinguished by being surrounded by the delimiting characters &lt; and &gt;.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f456647e-a469-4896-9c71-f16a274442b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The declared short name, if used, must precede the declared name.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c60586bf-91f1-440a-8825-43ae219dcc93"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> &lt;cyl&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cylinders; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part usage with the declared short name &lt;cyl&gt; and declared name cylinders</span> </span> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;'1'&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fullVehicleMassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassRequirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a requirement with the declared short name &lt;'1'&gt;, which is also considered its requirement ID. </span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Name and short name</strong><ul><li><span>If neither a declared name or a declared short name are given in the declaration of a usage, then it is determined by the name or short name of the redefined usage. Note that &lt;&gt; symbols should not be used when indicating the short name.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10206c6-2b45-46ba-8757-f4298c7bbc51"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;cyl&gt; </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cylinders</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Cylinder</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">..*];</span> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FourCylinderEngine</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cylinders</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its name is &quot;cylinders&quot;.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    // OR</span></span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cyl</span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is &quot;cyl&quot;. Note that &lt;&gt; symbols should not be used when indicating the short name.</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Specifying the declared name</h3><h4>Specifying the declared name via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the declared name via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword or declared short name with the needed basic or unrestricted name.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c015c5b9-69d7-444e-a6ec-20234b5554eb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Naming"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Naming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Naming"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing textual notation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span> </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25a5c11f-b1f9-4296-94e2-4196d26443bb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">LED Flashlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unrestricted element name that includes whitespace</span> </span> <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bulb</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">//<span> </span> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">basic element name without whitespace</span> </span> <br style="text-align: left;" />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying the declared name via the Specification Panel</h4><p>To specify the declared name via the Specification Panel</p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the declared name of the element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li></ul><h4>Specifying the declared name via the Containment tree</h4><p>To specify the declared name via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, double-click an element and type the needed name.</li></ul><h4>Specifying the declared name via the element symbol in a view</h4><p>To specify the declared name via the element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>On a symbol on view, double-click the name line under the element keyword and type the needed name.</li></ul><h3>Specifying the declared short name</h3><h4>Specifying the declared short name via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the declared short name via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword with the &lt;&gt; symbols with the needed short name within them.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span> </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="798a4155-8ca1-4b7f-b796-5cbea9e7b7b2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;cyl&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cylinders</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Cylinder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ..*];   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part usage with the declared short name &lt;cyl&gt; and declared name cylinders</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FourCylinderEngine</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cyl</span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is &quot;cyl&quot;. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005922 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x Refresh1) Element navigation

- page_id: `304005922`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005922/2026x+Refresh1+Element+navigation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:50.989+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Go To command in the element shortcut menu to navigate from one element to its related elements based on their relations. In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relations between elements, such as Flow, Connection, Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.

##### Navigating elements via the Go To command

To navigate elements via the Go To command

Do one of the following:

- Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the shortcut menu, click the Go To command, and in its submenu, click the needed element.
- Select an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the main menu, click Analyze , then Go To . In the submenu, click the needed element. The element is selected in the Containment tree. If the **Go To**submenu contains more than twenty elements, it displays the **Related Elements** command that opens a submenu with the full related elements list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the *vccu*element. Clicking the *'part vccu'* element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the *vccu*part element that subsets the *vccu*element for which the command was invoked.

##### Navigating elements via the Navigation tab

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relations between elements, such as Flow, Connection, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.</span></span></p><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Go To command</span></h3><p><span>To navigate elements via the Go To command</span></p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span>. In the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Go To </strong>command, and in its submenu, click the needed element. </li><li>Select an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span>. In the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong>, then <strong>Go To</strong>. In the submenu, click the needed element.<br />The element is selected in the Containment tree.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8b0775-8bcc-4cad-b27d-f5e759f45c18"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Go To </strong>submenu contains more than twenty elements, it displays the <strong>Related Elements</strong> command that opens a submenu with the full related elements list. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="go_to_command.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the <em>vccu </em>element. Clicking the <em>'part vccu'</em> element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the <em>vccu </em>part element that subsets the <em>vccu </em>element for which the command was invoked.  </h6><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Navigation tab</span></h3><ul><li><span>See the procedure </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating elements via the Navigation tab"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Navigating elements via the Navigation tab]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul>
