<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006279 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: (2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change

- page_id: `304006279`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006279/2026x+Refresh1+Element+ownership+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:19.742+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the procedures regarding element ownership change.

##### Changing element ownership

###### Changing element ownership via the Textual Editor

To change element ownership via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element notation you want to move, including its name and the closing semicolon or the contained body. partflashlight{// to remove this part element, select it along with its name and body contained within the curly braces 
partbulb;// to remove this part element, select the entire line 
}
2. Cut the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+X .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Cut .
3. Place the cursor within the curly braces of the intended owning element.
4. Paste the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+V .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Paste .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Changing element ownership via the Containment tree

To change element ownership via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, click the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element.

###### Changing element ownership via element symbol in a view

To change element ownership via element symbol in a view

1. In a view, click the symbol of the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the element symbol of the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element. Dragging and dropping the symbol itself changes the element's ownership which is different from the functionalities provided by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''].

###### Changing element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

To change element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon.
2. Click on the element you want to be the owner, then the element you want to designate as the owned one. A feature membership path is created between the elements.

###### Changing element ownership via the view palette

To change element ownership via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, under the Other Relationships group, select feature membership .
2. Draw a line with the diamond symbol at the end of the element symbol you want to be the owner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page outlines the procedures regarding element ownership change.</p><h3>Changing element ownership</h3><h4>Changing element ownership via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To change element ownership via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, select the element notation you want to move, including its name and the closing semicolon or the contained body.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="596cb013-deef-432d-8808-f0d286ce7534"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">flashlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// to remove this part element, select it along with its name and body contained within the curly braces</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bulb</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;          </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// to remove this part element, select the entire line </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Cut the selection in one of the following ways:<ol><li>On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+X</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Cut</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Place the cursor within the curly braces of the intended owning element.</li><li>Paste the selection in one of the following ways:<ol><li>On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+V</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Paste</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </li></ol><h4>Changing element ownership via the Containment tree</h4><p>To change element ownership via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, click the element whose owner you want to change.</li><li>Drag it onto the intended owner.<br />The element is moved to the new owning element.</li></ol><h4>Changing element ownership via element symbol in a view</h4><p>To change element ownership via element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, click the symbol of the element whose owner you want to change.</li><li>Drag it onto the element symbol of the intended owner.<br />The element is moved to the new owning element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35bc1e8a-1469-4079-b544-5abf0a5e73ca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging and dropping the symbol itself changes the element's ownership which is different from the functionalities provided by the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Changing element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To change element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>feature membership</strong> icon.</li><li>Click on the element you want to be the owner, then the element you want to designate as the owned one. A feature membership path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Changing element ownership via the view palette</h4><p>To change element ownership via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the View Palette, under the<span> </span>Other Relationships<strong><span> </span></strong>group, select <strong>feature membership</strong>. </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Draw a line with the diamond symbol at the end of the element symbol you want to be the owner.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005531 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00086: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling validation suites

- page_id: `304005531`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005531/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+validation+suites
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:44:14.395+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The model is only validated against validation suites enabled for the project. While predefined validation suites are active by default, you can choose which suites, including custom ones, should be activated or ignored.

There are two modes you can specify for the validation suites:

- Active . The validation suite is only run for active (automatically triggered) validation.
- Ignored . The validation suite is ignored for the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Enabling validation suites

Enabling a validation suite for the project via the **Project Validation Suites**dialog automatically creates a new package *ProjectValidationConfig* with the enabled project validation suites configuration. The package is created in a root namespace dedicated to configurations that activate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs' space=''], and validation suites. If no such namespace exists in the model, it is automatically created with the *ProjectValidationConfig*package. The root namespace's name depends on the name of the package first created in the namespace.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling validation suites via the Project Validation Suites dialog

To enable validation suites via the Project Validation Suites dialog

1. In the main toolbar, click Analyze > Validation > Project Validation Suites . The Project Validation Suites dialog opens.
2. To enable validation suites as active:
  1. In the Project Validation Suites dialog's Ignored section on the left, double-click or use the arrow buttons to add the needed validation suites to the Included section on the right.
3. To ignore validation suites:
  1. In the Project Validation Suites dialog's Included section on the right, double-click or use the arrow buttons to remove the needed validation suites, moving them to the Ignored section on the left.
4. Click the OK button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Project Validation Suites dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The model is only validated against validation suites enabled for the project. While predefined validation suites are active by default, you can choose which suites, including custom ones, should be activated or ignored. </p><p>There are two modes you can specify for the validation suites:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f8534331-96e8-4f2d-8185-6e60d84e2507"><strong>Active</strong>. The validation suite is only run for active (automatically triggered) validation.</li><li data-uuid="6540c3ab-98f0-4a10-a031-a6b78f119bc2"><strong>Ignored</strong>. The validation suite is ignored for the project.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enabling Validation Suites.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Enabling validation suites</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96d1a593-c17b-46f5-a4d1-463593cf1730"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling a validation suite for the project via the <strong>Project Validation Suites </strong>dialog automatically creates a new package <em>ProjectValidationConfig</em> with the enabled project validation suites configuration. The package is created in a root namespace dedicated to configurations that activate <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creation dialogs]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and validation suites. If no such namespace exists in the model, it is automatically created with the <em>ProjectValidationConfig </em>package. The root namespace's name depends on the name of the package first created in the namespace. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ProjectValidationConfig.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Enabling validation suites via the Project Validation Suites dialog </h4><p>To enable validation suites via the Project Validation Suites dialog </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="21ff1d4e-136c-4129-b6ea-f3b0d20fbc15">In the main toolbar, click <strong>Analyze </strong>&gt; <strong>Validation </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Validation Suites</strong>. The <strong>Project Validation Suites </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="f9cff871-6942-49ab-8721-ba3422e9e284">To enable validation suites as active:<ol><li data-uuid="655104f1-5fdf-44c1-929c-79632a95126a">In the <strong>Project Validation Suites </strong>dialog's <strong>Ignored</strong> section on the left, double-click or use the arrow buttons to add the needed validation suites to the <strong>Included </strong>section on the right.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="9c3b9a66-0c5c-48a5-9b6b-57c29d997336">To ignore validation suites:<ol><li data-uuid="c4f8ea43-f7d4-4866-8bc0-2ee348eb42a2">In the <strong>Project Validation Suites </strong>dialog's <strong>Included </strong>section on the right, double-click or use the arrow buttons to remove the needed validation suites, moving them to the <strong>Ignored </strong>section on the left.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="3ac47ec5-1e2f-4f52-8b3b-854241552e6b">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Validation Suites dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005969 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: (2026x Refresh1) Entry, do, and exit actions for states 

- page_id: `304005969`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005969/2026x+Refresh1+Entry+do+and+exit+actions+for+states%C2%A0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:53.746+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the *entry*, *do*, and *exit* actions.

##### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states

A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.

- An entry action starts when the state is activated.
- A do action starts after the entry action completes and continues while the state is active.
- An exit action starts when the state is exited, and the state becomes inactive once the exit action is completed.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the state's body, do one of the following:
  1. Define empty actions by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by a semicolon.
  2. Define actions within the state by declaring entry action/do action/exit action keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
  3. Specify actions referencing those defined elsewhere in the model by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by the name of the action you want to reference, then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateon{

// -------- Defining empty actions - Step 1.a 
entry; 
do; 
exit;

// -------- Defining actions within the state - Step 1.b

entryactionperformSelfTest{invehicle=operatingVehicle;} 
doactionprovidePower{} 
exitactionapplyParkingBrake:ApplyParkingBrake; // defining and feature typing actions within the state

// -------- Specifying actions that reference actions defined elsewhere - Step 1.c

entryperformSelfTest; 
doprovidePower; 
exitapplyParkingBrake; 
}

###### Specifying entry actions for states via the view palette

To specify entry actions for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click the entry **action**button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the entry action symbol.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a state symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
  - Select a state symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create Element button. In the displayed menu, select the needed action. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
3. In the **actions** compartment section's bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. In the shortcut menu, select the needed action. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the <em>entry</em>, <em>do</em>, and <em>exit</em> actions.</p><h3>Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states </h3><p>A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.</p><ul><li>An entry action starts when the state is the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a41a0a05-4dff-4d20-b128-1a7f3721498b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">--------</span> Defining actions within the state - Step 1.b</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">operatingVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">do</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span elsewhere - Step 1.c</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">entry</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006367 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00088: (2026x Refresh1) Enumeration

- page_id: `304006367`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006367/2026x+Refresh1+Enumeration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.027+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An enumeration definition's instances are limited to a set of enumeration usages, which are the enumerated values of the enumeration definition. An enumeration usage is defined by an enumeration definition and is restricted to only the set of enumerated values specified in the definition.

##### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements

###### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *enum def*for an enumeration definition.
  2. *enum* for an enumeration usage. The declaration of an enumerated value within the body of an enumeration definition may omit the *enum* keyword.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. attributedefConditionLevel{ 
attributecolor:ConditionColor; 
}enumdefConditionColor{red;green;yellow; } 
// OR 
enumdefConditionColor{ 
enumred; 
enumgreen; 
enumyellow; 
} 
 
enumdefRiskLevel:>ConditionLevel{ 
enumlow{:>>color=ConditionColor::green; } 
enummedium{:>>color=ConditionColor::yellow; } 
enumhigh{:>>color=ConditionColor::red; } 
}

###### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create enumeration usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. enum def to create an enumeration definition element.
  2. enum to create an enumeration usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.

###### Creating enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an enumeration definition element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the enum element.
  - Select an enumeration symbol and on the right side of the enums compartment, click the Create enum button. The new element is created and displayed in the enums compartment.

###### Creating enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel

To create enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] of an enumeration definition.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **enum**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **enums**compartment section's bar, click the **Create enum**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **enums** compartment.

###### Creating enumeration definition elements via the view palette

To create enumeration definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **enum** def button to create an enumeration definition element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the enumeration symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An enumeration definition's instances are limited to a set of enumeration usages, which are the enumerated values of the enumeration definition. An enumeration usage is defined by an enumeration definition and is restricted to only the set of enumerated values specified in the definition.</p><h3>Creating enumeration usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="759232a9-1a6c-4db8-a45b-beeecdfd7ca0">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword: <ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>enum def</em><span> </span>for an enumeration definition.</p></li><li><span> <em>enum</em></span><em><span> </span></em>for an enumeration usage.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="795125b2-11b4-4ce8-9d31-3b5d22fb383f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The declaration of an enumerated value within the body of an enumeration definition may omit the <em>enum</em> keyword. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="9bec086d-a417-4f96-839a-bc5d91bc95b7">Specify the element name.</li><li data-uuid="36d3c090-1f64-4d54-b6dd-837d4afa449c">(Optional) Specify the enumeration's features in the element body.</li><li data-uuid="64dd538b-5454-431a-a2be-713fa93d7e34">Click right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="b3834a7a-c23c-48b4-8007-ab61338d093e">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>enum def</strong> to create an enumeration definition element.</li><li><strong>enum </strong>to create an enumeration usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="654527fc-3acf-4633-b7f4-6fe2cee0ef22">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="99034554-b796-4016-a559-0eba478651b6">(Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.</li></ol><h4>Creating enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /></ac:link> page.</p><p>To create enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="90a2c828-ac48-40d4-b15c-77e5c5549b4b">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select an enumeration definition element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>enum </strong>element.</li><li>Select an enumeration symbol and on the right side of the <strong>enums </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>enum </span></strong>button. <br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>enums </strong>compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Creating enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="60fd1cab-7d03-4ec5-8de3-abbac582bda9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of an enumeration definition.</span></li><li data-uuid="585217e5-9e35-4837-ad64-70fc3eea344d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>enum </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>enums </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create enum </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>enums</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Creating enumeration definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create enumeration definition elements via the view palette </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9f2511b4-2160-4cf2-a369-cd38451497b7">In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the<span> <strong>enum </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create an enumeration definition element.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the enumeration symbol.</li><li data-uuid="f5b74fb4-2aac-496b-a744-55bcf0715733">(Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006164 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: (2026x Refresh1) Environment options

- page_id: `304006164`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006164/2026x+Refresh1+Environment+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:08.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides information on the SysML v2-specific environment options you can use to customize your workflow.

##### Opening the Environment Options dialog

To open the **Environment Options** dialog

- From the Options menu, select Environment . Then select either SysML v2 or SysML v2 Textual Editor group. If you cannot see the SysML v2 groups in the dialog, ensure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Switching to System Engineer SysML v2 perspective' space='']switched the modeling tool's perspective to **System Engineer SysML v2**.

##### Modifying environment options

To modify environment options

1. Open the **Environment Options** dialog .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick filter' space='MT'] to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the environment option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### SysML v2 Environment Options

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Show Element Keyword | true | true/false | Set to true to display element keywords (e.g., part, action, actor) across the UI, such as in the Containment tree, Specification panel, and shortcut menu, while excluding views. |
| SysML v2 Textual Editor |  |  |  |
| Code Style |  |  |  |
| Add Blank Lines Between Textual Notation Blocks | false | true/false | Set to true to insert a blank line after each textual notation block in the textual notation to separate the blocks. |
| Display Specializations as Keywords | false | true/false | Set to true to display specializations as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. >]]> |
| Display Metadata as Keywords | false | true/false | Set to true to display metadata as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. >]]> |
| Use Short Name Instead of Name | true | true/false | Set to true to display the element's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. >]]>This option applies only when an element is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting an element in a view). If the element is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the element is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target element will be displayed. |
| Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement | false | true/false | Set to true to display the requirement's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. >]]>This option applies only when a requirement is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting a requirement in a view). If the requirement is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the requirement is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target requirement will be displayed. |
| Max Column for Hard Wrap | 170 | Number | Specify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords ("connect", "from", "to", "of"), binary operators ("+", "-"), commas, and long single-line bodies. Set to -1 to disable the max column. |
| Editor |  |  |  |
| Synchronize Model with Errors | false | true/false | Set to true to synchronize the model with the textual notation, even if the notation contains errors. >]]> |
| Run Validation | true | true/false | Set to true to validate the model and display validation failures in the Textual Editor. >]]> |
| Force KerML Namespaces Opening | false | true/false | Set to true to force the opening of KerML namespaces in the Textual Editor. |
| Preferred Element Name for Autocompletion | Short name | Short nameNameBoth names | Specify which element name should be suggested in context-sensitive autocompletion. If the selected name type is not specified for an element, autocompletion will suggest an existing name type instead. >]]> |
| Enable Sticky Header | true | true/false | Set to true to enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor, which displays the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. >]]> |
| Sticky Header Line Count | 5 | Number | Specify the maximum number of lines to display in the sticky header. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. >]]> |
| Font Size | 14 | Number | Specify the default font size. |
| Enable Minimap | true | true/false | Set to true to show the minimap in the top-right corner of the Textual Editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. >]]> |
| Name Resolution |  |  |  |
| Name Resolution Error Limit | 10 | Number | Specify the name resolution error limit to control the handling of name resolution errors. When specified, the name resolution stops if the error limit is reached, leaving the remaining names unresolved. This limit applies independently to each open Textual Editor tab. Set to -1 to disable the error limit and allow name resolution to continue for all names, skipping only those with errors. >]]> |
| Name Resolution Mode | Fast | FastBalancedFull Precision | Specify the name resolution mode. Select 'Fast' for maximum speed with rare corner-case inaccuracies. Select 'Balanced' for a reliable compromise between performance and accuracy. Select 'Full Precision' for guaranteed accuracy with slower execution. >]]> |
| Text Generation |  |  |  |
| Smart Generation Timeout | -1.0 | -1.0 - ∞ | Specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Set to -1 to disable the timeout. >]]> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page provides information on the SysML v2-specific environment options you can use to customize your workflow.</p><h3>Opening the Environment Options dialog</h3><p>To open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. <strong>Show Description</strong> button to display the environment option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Show /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to <em>true</em>/<em>false</em>, or selecting a value from the list.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f945ab-1085-4e2c-9a06-d8bd3009f2a3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> However, if the element is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target element will be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set to true to display the requirement's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element names" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e53dfe2e-f05e-49c7-9fde-d35817f02e5b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option applies only when a requirement is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting a requirement in a view). If the requirement is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the requirement is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target requirement will be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Max Column for Hard Wrap</td><td>170</td><td>Number</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords (&quot;connect&quot;, &quot;from&quot;, &quot;to&quot;, &quot;of&quot;), binary operators (&quot;+&quot;, &quot;-&quot;), commas, and long single-line bodies. Set to -1 to disable the max column.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background color : " colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Editor</td></tr><tr><td>Synchronize Model with Errors</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to synchronize the model with the textual notation, even if the notation contains errors. <ac:link ac:anchor="Saving changes in the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td>Run Validation</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to validate the model and display validation failures in the Textual Editor. <ac:link ac:anchor="Validation Results pop-up in Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing validation results" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td>Force KerML Namespaces Opening</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to force the opening of KerML namespaces in the Textual Editor.</td></tr><tr><td>Preferred Element Name for Autocompletion</td><td>Short name</td><td><p>Short name</p><p>Name</p><p>Both names</p></td><td>Specify which element name should be suggested in context-sensitive autocompletion. If the selected name type is not specified for an element, autocompletion will suggest an existing name type instead. <ac:link ac:anchor="Autocompletion"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual Editor features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Enable Sticky Header</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor, which displays the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td>Sticky Header Line Count</td><td>5</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum number of lines to display in the sticky header. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Font Size</td><td>14</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the default font size.</td></tr><tr><td>Enable Minimap</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to show the minimap in the top-right corner of the Textual Editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Name Resolution</td></tr><tr><td>Name Resolution Error Limit</td><td>10</td><td>Number</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Specify the name resolution error limit to control the handling of name resolution errors. When specified, the name resolution stops if the error limit is reached, leaving the remaining names unresolved. This limit applies independently to each open Textual Editor tab. Set to -1 to disable the error limit and allow name resolution to continue for all names, skipping only those with errors. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></td></tr><tr><td>Name Resolution Mode</td><td>Fast</td><td><p>Fast</p><p>Balanced</p><p>Full Precision</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Specify the name resolution mode. Select 'Fast' for maximum speed with rare corner-case inaccuracies. Select 'Balanced' for a reliable compromise between performance and accuracy. Select 'Full Precision' for guaranteed accuracy with slower execution. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background color : " colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Text Generation</td></tr><tr><td>Smart Generation Timeout </td><td>-1.0</td><td>-1.0 - ∞</td><td>Specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Set to -1 to disable the timeout. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Textual notation import/export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005933 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: (2026x Refresh1) Exhibit state

- page_id: `304005933`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005933/2026x+Refresh1+Exhibit+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:51.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An exhibit state usage specifies that its owner exhibits a state specified by the exhibit state usage.

An exhibit state usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced state is exhibited by the containing part.
- a state definition or usage, whereby the exhibit state usage represents a "call" from the containing state to the exhibited state.

There are two ways a state can be exhibited by another element:

50%

- by referencing the state usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the exhibit keyword (unnamed element) or the exhibit state keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_state_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned exhibit state usage, i.e., the exhibited state is the exhibit state usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the exhibit state keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_state_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Exhibiting a state by referencing a state usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor1Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor

To exhibit states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to exhibit a state, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed exhibit state, declare the exhibit keyword followed by the name of the exhibited state.
  2. For a named one, declare the exhibit state keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the exhibited state.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

state vehicleStates; // referenced state

partvehicle{ 
// unnamed exhibit state referencing a state defined elsewhere in the model 
exhibitvehicleStates;

// named exhibit state referencing a state defined elsewhere in the modelexhibitstateoperatingStatereferences vehicleStates;// ORexhibitstateoperatingState::> vehicleStates;

}

###### Exhibiting states via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To exhibit states via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag a state element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to exhibit the state by referencing it using the **Create Exhibit State** command.
  - the exhibit state usage symbol (keyword exhibit state ) that you want to exhibit the state by referencing it using the **Set Exhibited State** command.

###### Exhibiting states via the view palette

To exhibit states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click exhibit . If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the**exhibit state******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To exhibit an existing state, in the view pane, click on the source element symbol, then on the state element symbol.
  2. To exhibit a new state, in the view pane, click on the source element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a state element symbol. The exhibit path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Exhibiting statesvia the Create Relation command

To exhibit states via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click exhibit . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Exhibiting statesvia the Specification panel

To exhibit states via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a state element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Exhibited State** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the exhibit state element. The appropriate state is specified for the element.

##### Exhibiting a statevia the ownedexhibit stateusageitselfas theexhibited state

###### Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor

To exhibit states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to exhibit a state, declare the exhibit state keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle{

// exhibited state is the defined exhibit state usage itself contained by the parentexhibitstate vehicleStates;}

###### Exhibiting states via the Create Element command

To exhibit states via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the exhibit state element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the exhibit state's features.

###### Exhibiting states via the view palette2Exhibiting states via the view palette

To exhibit states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click the exhibit state button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the exhibit state symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the exhibit state's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">exhibit</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleStates</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Exhibiting states via the Create Element command</h4><p>To exhibit states via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>exhibit state </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the exhibit state's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="572b545d-6337-4a14-b497-9d6e2aa90456"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Exhibiting states via the view palette2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Exhibiting states via the view palette</h4><p>To exhibit states via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the States group, click the <strong>exhibit state </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the exhibit state symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the exhibit state's features.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005098 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00091: (2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views

- page_id: `304005098`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005098/2026x+Refresh1+Exposing+elements+for+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:07.073+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating views' space=''], you can specify what model elements can be displayed by the view using the expose relationship. The exposed elements can be further narrowed down using[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views' space=''].

Elements can be exposed for views only via the Textual Editor. For displaying exposed elements on views, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in views' space=''] page.

##### Element exposition kinds and visibility

Expose relationship is a kind of import relationship and is thus declared like an import relationship, but using the keyword *expose* instead of *import*. However, unlike imports, whose visibility can be modified, expose relationships always have *protected* visibility, which means that the elements exposed by a view usage are not publicly visible outside the view usage.

The exposition kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the exposed elements qualified name.

| Exposition kind | Suffix | Exposed elements | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership exposition | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // membership exposition: expose vehicleInformation; // exposes vehicleInformation} |
| Namespace exposition | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // namespace exposition: expose vehicleInformation::*; // exposes vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord} |
| Recursive membership exposition | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive membership exposition: expose vehicleInformation::**; // exposes vehicleInformation, vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |
| Recursive namespace exposition | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | part vehicleInformation { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}view 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive namespace exposition: expose vehicleInformation::*::**; // exposes vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |

##### Exposing elements

To expose elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view element for which you want to expose the element(s).
2. Declare the keyword expose .
3. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the .
4. (Optional) Specify an element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

You can specify multiple *expose* relationships for the same view.

partvehicleInformation{partvehicleSpecs{}partvehicleRecord{itemvehicleModel;}}

view'Vehicle Details':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv,DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{exposevehicleInformation::**;// recursive membership exposition of *vehicleInformation*, *vehicleSpecs*, *vehicleRecord*, *vehicleModel*}

#### NOTE: Reviewing exposed elements

Reviewing exposed elements

To review the elements exposed for a specific element, open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']**Specification** window and check the values of the **Exposed Element**property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">vehicleInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// recursive membership exposition of <em>vehicleInformation</em>, <em>vehicleSpecs</em>, <em>vehicleRecord</em>, <em>vehicleModel<br /></em></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="287bb803-6eab-403f-9bc4-51293fb916ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Reviewing exposed elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To review the elements exposed for a specific element, open its <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification window"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link> and check the values of the <strong>Exposed Element </strong>property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006207 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: (2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage

- page_id: `304006207`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006207/2026x+Refresh1+Extract+Definition+and+Usage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:10.945+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Element management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Extract Definition and Extract Usage commands improve feature reusability when the model grows to contain usage elements with repetitive features.

- The Extract Definition commands allow you to extract a definition element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate definition elements that can be used for typing other usage elements.
- The Extract Usage commands allow you to extract a usage element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate action, use case, and stateusage elements that can be reused and adapted to different contexts.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']On the left: the same attributes *diameter*and *width* are each created for the parts *leftFrontWheel*and *rightFrontWheel*, resulting in duplicated elements that serve the same purpose. 
On the right: once the Extract Definition command **Extract All Features** is executed on the *rightFrontWheel*element, the definition is extracted with the part's attributes. 
The *rightFrontWheel*element is then defined by the extracted definition, inheriting its attributes. The *leftFrontWheel*part can then be defined by the definition as well.

##### Extract Definition commands

Extract Definition commands are:

- Extract All Features to extract a definition element with all of the usage's features.
- Extract Only Attributes and Parameters to extract a definition element only with attributes and parameters of the usage.

Once you execute the Extract Definition commands:

- The features, except for the redefined ones, are moved to the definition, which is then set as the type for the usage. All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of being owned by the usage.

To extract a definition element from a usage element

- Right-click a usage element(s) or its symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then in the Extract Definition group, click one of the following:
  - Extract All Features . The appropriate definition element is created.
  - Extract Only Attributes and Parameters . The appropriate definition element is created only with the extracted attributes and parameters.

##### Extract Usage commands

You can also use the Extract Usage commands:

- Extract All Features to extract a usage element with all of the usage's features.
- Extract Only Attributes and Parameters to extract a usage element only with attributes and parameters of the usage.

The commands are especially useful when reusing and adapting [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling actions with swimlanes' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Use case scenario' space=''], and states to different contexts.

Once you execute the Extract Usage commands:

- The extracted usage is owned by the same package and named after the element from which it was extracted;
- The extracting usage reference subsets the extracted usage;
- All owned features of the extracting usage are moved to the extracted usage, except for the redefined ones;
- All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of owned by the extracting usage.

The **Extract Usage**commands are only available for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Perform action' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Include use case' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exhibit state' space=''], unless they reference other elements.

To extract a usage element from a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element

- Right-click a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element(s) or their symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then in the Extract Usage group, click one of the following:
  - Extract All Features . The appropriate action, use case, or state usage element is created.
  - Extract Only Attributes and Parameters . The appropriate action, use case, or state usage element is created only with the extracted attributes and parameters.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Extract Definition and Extract Usage commands improve feature reusability when the model grows to contain usage elements with repetitive features. </span></p><ul><li data-uuid="11d1965f-bcbf-490d-ae24-f9754c54a996"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Extract Definition commands allow you to extract a definition element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate definition elements that can be used for typing other usage elements.</span></li><li data-uuid="c8954c03-c306-4804-83c3-e7710c92c8f7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Extract Usage commands allow you to extract a usage element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">action, use case, and state </span>usage elements that can be reused and adapted to different contexts.</span></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_definition.png" /></ac:image>On the left: the same attributes <em>diameter </em>and <em>width</em> are each created for the parts <em>leftFrontWheel </em>and <em>rightFrontWheel</em>, resulting in duplicated elements that serve the same purpose.<br />On the right: once the Extract Definition command <strong>Extract All Features</strong> is executed on the <em>rightFrontWheel </em>element, the definition is extracted with the part's attributes. <br />The <em>rightFrontWheel </em>element is then defined by the extracted definition, inheriting its attributes. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004952 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00093: (2026x Refresh1) Extracting style sheets

- page_id: `304004952`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004952/2026x+Refresh1+Extracting+style+sheets
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:27:29.232+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In a view displaying elements with applied [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metadata' space=''], you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. The extracted style sheet is automatically applied to the view and the displayed symbols. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Extracting style sheets

To extract style sheets via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

1. Open a view displaying elements with applied metadata.
2. Click the Style Sheets [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click the **Extract and** Apply Style Sheet command. The Extract Style Sheets dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Extract Style Sheets.png']
3. In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet.
4. Click Extract .

A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its symbol is displayed in the view. The new styles are applied to the displayed symbols based on the applied metadata feature value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Extracting style sheet rules

If you have already created a style sheet, you can supplement its existing rules by extracting metadata-based rules.

To extract a style sheet rule via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

1. Open a view displaying elements with applied metadata.
2. In a view, select the style sheet's part definition element symbol and click the Create Element button on it.
3. In the dropdown menu, click on the extract rule . The Extract Rule dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract rule.png']
4. In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet rule.
5. Click Extract .

A rule is extracted in the selected style sheet and applied to element symbols based on the applied metadata feature value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the dropdown menu, click the<span><span> </span><strong>Extract and<span> </span></strong></span><strong>Apply Style Sheet<span> </span></strong>command. The <strong>Extract <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="074e1482-773c-4def-aa74-7e3f86251236">Style Sheets </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="074e1482-773c-4def-aa74-7e3f86251236">dialog opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract Style Sheets.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="0af1b89f-9fa7-4c25-951f-38a10868e64d">In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet.</li><li data-uuid="e52ace7d-b33f-4f9d-b7ca-dd467cb8a3fe">Click <strong>Extract</strong>.</li></ol><p>A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its symbol is displayed in the view. The new styles are applied to the displayed symbols based <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ad6fda1d-b585-409f-87c2-5a8277c4fffc">on the applied metadata feature value.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extracted Style Sheets.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Extracting style sheet rules</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If you have already created a style sheet, you can supplement its existing rules by extracting metadata-based rules. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To extract a style sheet rule via the Style Sheet symbol compartment</span></p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="969475ab-e3e3-4434-b1de-96d4a7299744">Open a view displaying elements with applied metadata.</li><li data-uuid="e5895d57-2b1b-432b-88fc-10b8a90615f6">In a view, select the style sheet's part definition element symbol and click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button on it.</li><li data-uuid="05c1fe5a-593a-4ce4-9f22-ca40ed9ea0f1">In the dropdown menu, click on the <strong>extract rule</strong>. The <strong>Extract Rule </strong>dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract rule.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="6431f35d-4bf7-4ea7-a862-4569c1a98038">In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet rule.</li><li data-uuid="3ca6bb4f-b232-4656-84bb-be19273b3b91">Click <strong>Extract</strong>.</li></ol><p>A rule is extracted in the selected style sheet and applied to element symbols <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3a772a74-0631-4444-8e49-853765ac681d">based on the applied metadata feature value.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extracting style sheet rules.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006381 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2026x Refresh1) Feature membership

- page_id: `304006381`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006381/2026x+Refresh1+Feature+membership
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:27.463+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use feature memberships to specify element features.

- Feature membership relationship is created automatically in the tool when you create a feature for an element.
  - For element creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements' space=''] page. You can also create features through the creation of a feature membership itself. This is especially useful for element decomposition, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Part decomposition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Requirement decomposition' space=''].
- For element ownership change procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element ownership change' space=''] page.
- The feature membership display can differ based on the related elements. If one of the elements related via a feature membership is a referential usage, it is displayed with a hollow diamond icon, whereas a composite usage is displayed with a filled diamond icon. For procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers' space=''] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying feature memberships

###### Specifying feature memberships via the Textual Editor

To specify feature memberships via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, use curly braces after element declaration to specify the element body. Features specified within the element body become related to the owning element via the feature membership. To indicate a referential (non-composite) usage, use the keyword *ref*before the element keyword. For more procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers' space=''] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part'input unit'{ 
partmouse;// composite usagerefpartmonitor;// referential (non-composite) usage}

###### Specifying feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating features via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., parts, requirements).

To specify feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part for which you want to specify a feature membership.
2. In the selected symbol's smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon and do one of the following:
  1. To create a composite feature, click elsewhere in the view pane. The new composite feature of the same type is created.
  2. To designate an existing element as a composite one, click on the element you want to designate as the composite feature.

###### Specifying feature memberships via the view palette

To specify feature memberships via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **feature membership** button.
2. In a view, click on the element symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned feature. A feature membership path symbol is created between the elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use feature memberships to specify element features.</p><ul><li>Feature membership relationship is created automatically in the tool when you create a feature for an element.<ul><li>For element creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing elements" /></ac:link> page.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7707921-3921-43e6-a0e3-9c8ab5c299f5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create features through the creation of a feature membership itself. Features specified within the element body become related to the owning element via the feature membership.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffb2b701-0420-436d-91e8-7584fd12f900"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To indicate a referential (non-composite) usage, use the keyword <em>ref </em>before the element keyword. For more procedures on referential usages, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying the ref modifier for features"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specifying the ref modifier for features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b03f9e37-c62c-418a-b5a6-7c4691588b8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'input unit'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mouse</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite usage<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">monitor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential (non-composite) usage<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>Creating features via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., parts, requirements).</p><p>To specify feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part for which you want to specify a feature membership.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the selected symbol's smart manipulator, click the</span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span> </span></strong><strong>feature membership</strong> <span>icon and do one of the following:</span><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">To create a composite feature, </ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">click elsewhere in the view pane. The new composite feature of the same type is created.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">To designate an existing element as a composite one, </ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="47f0256c-a451-4389-8d47-12c211b97602">click on the element you want to designate as the composite feature.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Specifying feature memberships via the view palette</h4><p>To specify feature memberships via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the<span> <strong>feature membership </strong></span><span>button.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the element symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned feature. A feature membership path symbol is created between the elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005774 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: (2026x Refresh1) Feature typing

- page_id: `304005774`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005774/2026x+Refresh1+Feature+typing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:45.530+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations > (2026x Refresh1) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use thefeature typing relationship tospecify a definition for a usage.

##### Feature typing

###### Defining an element via the Textual Editor

To define an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to define.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword defined by
  2. textual symbol : (colon)
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the definition element you want to be the defining one.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdieselEngine:Engine; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEnginedefined byEngine;

###### Defining an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To define an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Type command.

###### Defining an element via the view palette

To define an element via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select feature typing .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the element you want to be defined to the defining one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Defining an element via the Create Relation command

To define an elementvia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to define.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click feature typing . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the defining one. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the defining one and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the defining one from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Defining an element via the Specification panel

To define an element via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
