<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005083 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: (2026x Refresh1) Filtering elements for views

- page_id: `304005083`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005083/2026x+Refresh1+Filtering+elements+for+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:05.791+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify filters for view definitions and/or specific views to narrow down what elements can be displayed by views. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating views' space=''], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exposing elements for views' space=''], you can then optionally filter the exposed elements. Views have the capability to filter elements upon exposition based on certain conditions.Only elements that meet all filter conditions are exposed for the view.

Elements can be filtered for views only via the Textual Editor.

##### Filtering elements for views

Ways to perform element filtering for views:

- . You can specify filter conditions directly for the specific view, using the keyword filter .
- . You can also filter elements for views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains filter conditions, the filters are inherited by and applied to the defined view.

###### Specifying filter conditions

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space='']) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Views basics' space=''] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return *true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in the used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).
- Useful filter: *filter KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement == false;* which filters out KerML library elements.

To specify filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to expose .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

view'Vehicle Features'{ 
exposeVehicle::**;// exposes package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations: 
filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// exposes part usage elements from the package *Vehicle* 
filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// exposes elements with *Safety* metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements exposed from the package *Vehicle* 
}

###### Inheriting filter conditions

**Filters in predefined view definitions:**

- Standard predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views' space='']/[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space=''] view definitions (except for *RequirementsTable*) do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify filters to limit the displayed elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views' space=''] contain:
  - Element filters specified in the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter*view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.
  - Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., *PartsTreeView*view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.

**Recommendations**:

- The easiest way to filter exposed elements is to reuse predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions. See *Example 1*.
  - Keep in mind that the renderers specified for the view definitions are also inherited.
- If you only want to discard uncommon library elements from being displayed in a view, you can reuse the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter***view definitions. See *Example 2*.

#### PANEL: Example 1

Example 1

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
} 
viewdefTreeView :> EssentialElementsFilter, NonStandardLibraryElementFilter { // the *TreeView*view definition subclassifies the two view definitions inheriting their filters 
renderViews::asTreeDiagram; // the specified renderer will be inherited 
}

viewdefPartsTreeView :> TreeView { // the *PartsTreeView*view definition subclassifies *TreeView* inheriting all filters and renderer 
filterhastypePartUsage; 
}

viewnewView:DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView{ // the specific view is defined by *PartsTreeView*view definition inheriting all filters and renderer 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

#### PANEL: Example 2

Example 2

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
}

viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{ // the specific view is defined by the view definitions inheriting their filters 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

// the view is defined by the *Interconnection View* view definition (inheriting its renderer) and view definitions specifying filters:viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViews::iv, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{exposeVehicle::**;}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span elements exposed from the package <em>Vehicle</em><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Inheriting filter conditions</h4><p><strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filters in predefined view definitions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span></strong></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b0a4d63-99a1-4a64-896b-f8c377d23d09">Standard</ac:inline-comment-marker> predefined <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbolic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>/<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view definitions (except for <em>RequirementsTable</em>) do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify filters to limit the displayed elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> contain:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Element filters specified in the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter </em>and <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter </em>view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Recommendations</strong>:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The easiest way to filter exposed elements is to reuse predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions. See <em>Example 1</em>. </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Keep in mind that the renderers specified for the view definitions are also inherited.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If you only want to </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">discard uncommon library elements from being displayed in a view, you can reuse the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter </em>and <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter</em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em> </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions.</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See <em>Example 2</em>.</span></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03aba2d0-8f56-4f45-8751-79ebf52d04cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> NonStandardLibraryElementFilter {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   // see the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</em> view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> TreeView :&gt; EssentialElementsFilter, NonStandardLibraryElementFilter { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>TreeView </em>view definition subclassifies the two view definitions inheriting their filters</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Views::asTreeDiagram; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specified renderer will be inherited</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> PartsTreeView :&gt; TreeView { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition subclassifies <em>TreeView</em> inheriting all filters and renderer</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">hastype</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> PartUsage;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition inheriting all filters and renderer</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed62dd3-00e8-480d-ba31-7f21b2afce28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> NonStandardLibraryElementFilter {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   // see the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</em> view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> new</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">View</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definitions inheriting their filters</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view is defined by the <em>Interconnection View</em> view definition (inheriting its renderer) and view definitions specifying filters:<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::iv</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004772 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: (2026x Refresh1) Find dialog

- page_id: `304004772`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004772/2026x+Refresh1+Find+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:44.923+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor > (2026x Refresh1) Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation.

##### Invoking the Find dialog

To invoke the Find dialog

- Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.

##### Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, enter the word you are searching for. The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.

##### Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation.

To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, input the word you want to replace.
3. On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.
4. In the Replace field, input the replacement word.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the Replace button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.
  2. To replace all instances of the word, either click the **Replace** **All**button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard. All instances of the word can also be changed via the **Change All Occurrences**(Ctrl+F2) command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><h3>Invoking the Find dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To invoke the Find dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.</li></ul><h3>Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, enter the word you are searching for.<br />The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.</li></ol><h3>Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><p>To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, input the word you want to replace.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.</li><li>In the <strong>Replace</strong> field, input the replacement word.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To replace all instances of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> <strong>All </strong>button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a00e8947-6778-4457-a9d6-a064b6f6f518"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All instances of the word can also be changed via the <strong>Change All Occurrences </strong>(Ctrl+F2) command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005744 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: (2026x Refresh1) Flow

- page_id: `304005744`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005744/2026x+Refresh1+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:44.999+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Relations > (2026x Refresh1) Connectors
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. The transferred payload can be anything (attribute value, item, part, etc.).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays the *fuelFlow* flow connectorfrom *fuelTank.fuelOut*to*engine.fuelIn*with the payload *flowingFuel*defined by *Fuel*.Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating flow usage and definition elements

###### Creating flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the flow keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] (optional), (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the source element and output feature. Then follow with the keyword to and the name of the target element and input feature. If the flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword *from*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow fuelFlow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // named and specialized (feature typing) flowflow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // unnamed specialized(feature typing) flowflow fuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn;// named unspecializedflowflowfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unnamed unspecializedflow can omit the keyword *from*// alternative notation for creating a flow without the source input and output, only source and target: 
partvehicle{ 
partengine{ 
outportfuelIn; 
} 
partfuelTank{ 
inportfuelOut; 
} 
flow{ 
end::>engine; 
end::>fuelTank{ 
:>>fuelOut; 
} 
} 
}

###### Creating flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the flow command.
2. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
3. Click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements. Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the **flow** command's dropdown menu, you can select **None**. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.

###### Creating flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

To create flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

- To create a flow definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** def button to create a flow definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the flow definition symbol to be created.
- To create a flow usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** button to create a flow usage.
  2. In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
  3. Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **flow**or the **flow****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the **flow** command's dropdown menu, you can select **None**. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the *flow* def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or specialization of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow def FuelFlow{ 
ref :>>payload:Fuel; 
 end portfuelIn:FuelIn; 
 end portfuelOut:FuelOut; 
}

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command

To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click flow def for flow definition.

###### Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

##### Setting the payload for the flow

While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the **Show Payload** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword*from*within the flow for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow:FuelFlowofFuelfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with payload set as a definition elementflow: FuelFlowof flowingFuel :FuelfromfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with a named and specialized (feature typing) payloadflow of Fuel from fuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unspecialized flowwith payload set as a definition element

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on:
  - a flow element symbol executing the **Set Payload** **** command.
  - a binary connection path executing the **Create Flow** **** command to create a flow and to set the payload.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. The transferred payload can be anything (attribute value, item, part, etc.).</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_flow.png" /></ac:image><span>The image displays the <em>fuelFlow</em> flow connector </span>from <em>fuelTank.fuelOut</em><span> to </span><em>engine.fuelIn </em><span>with the payload <em>flowingFuel </em>defined by <em>Fuel</em>. </span><span>Additionally, the connector is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h3>Creating flow usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating flow usage elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create flow usage elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>flow </em>keyword. </li><li>Specify the element name (optional), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional), <ac:link ac:anchor="Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[payload]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional), then the <em>from </em>keyword and the name of the source element and output feature. Then follow with the keyword <em>to </em>and the name of the target element and input feature. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="242ae7b3-a28c-4bb8-aa32-a6c470c46322"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>from </em>can be omitted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de1dc05f-b34f-4963-b560-25ddf828339d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelFlow</span></span></span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named and specialized (feature typing) flow<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow</span><span> </span></span>:<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;                   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelFlow</span></span></span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named un<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specialized </span></span>flow<br /></span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                                           <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed un<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specialized </span></span>flow can omit the keyword <em>from</em></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// alternative notation for creating a flow without the source input and output, only source and target:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">out</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;        </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>flow </strong>command.</li><li>A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.</li><li>Click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f09c891-fbe6-403d-9954-b2229a417dbe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the <strong>flow</strong> command's dropdown menu, you can select <strong>None</strong>. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow definition and usage elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create flow definition and usage elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create a flow definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>flow </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create a flow definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the flow definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create a flow usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>flow </strong></span><span>button to create a flow usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.</li><li>Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="29563be4-2b9c-4dbb-a777-9255e98a7768">If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>flow </strong></span>or the <span><strong>flow </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</li><li data-uuid="cfbcc360-0a21-4f1b-987e-293f572f7bac">Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the <strong>flow</strong> command's dropdown menu, you can select <strong>None</strong>. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4>Creating flow definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create flow definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the<span> <em>flow </em></span><em>def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or specialization of the needed element. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   ref</span> :&gt;&gt;<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">payload</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>flow def</strong> for flow definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h3>Setting the payload for the flow</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b2449c9-18d1-417d-a7a1-208643954cb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the <strong>Show Payload</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To set the payload for the flow via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">before the keyword<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">from<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">within the flow for which you want to set a payload.</span></p></li><li>Declare the keyword <em>of </em>followed by either of the following:<ol><li>The name of the payload specialized by a definition element. </li><li>The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ebeef5-7016-4332-ba47-0dc7e6fe8373"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow with payload set as a definition element<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow </span>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">flowingFuel :</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span></span>;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow with a named and specialized (feature typing) <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">payload </span></span></span></span></span><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;                                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unspecialized flow<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> with payload <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">set as a definition element</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Setting the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To set the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on:</span><ul><li data-uuid="773c3df5-6552-4ff2-9d8f-abd65fe0ca03"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a flow <span>element symbol executing the <strong>Set Payload</strong></span></span><span><strong> </strong></span>command.</li><li data-uuid="9a07cec0-f0f6-4567-9b35-e1207914b5d6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a binary connection <span>path executing the <strong>Create Flow</strong></span></span><span><strong> </strong></span>command to create a flow and to set the payload.</li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005717 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: (2026x Refresh1) Frame concern

- page_id: `304005717`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005717/2026x+Refresh1+Frame+concern
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:44.361+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements > (2026x Refresh1) Concerns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A framed concern usage is a subrequirement usage, which will automatically be considered a required constraint of its containing requirement definition or usage.

There are two ways concerns can be framed:

50%

- by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame keyword (unnamed element) or the frame concern keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned frame concern usage, i.e., the framed concern is the frame concern usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame concern keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='frame concern 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Framing a concern by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed frame concern, declare the frame keyword followed by the name of the framed concern.
  2. For a named frame concern, declare the frame concern keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reference subsetting' space=''] the framed concern.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

concernbrakingConcern{subjectvehicle:Vehicle;stakeholderdriver:Person;attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;}

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

framebrakingConcern{ // step 1.a - unnamed usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
}

frame concernbrakingRequirementConcern ::> brakingConcern{ // step 1.b - named usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
 :>> maxBrakingDistance = brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
 } 
}

###### Framing concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To frame concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the requirement element symbol that you want to frame a concern by referencing it using the Create Frame Concern command.
  - the frame concern usage symbol that you want to frame a concern by referencing it using the **Set Framed Concern** command.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the Framed Concern field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the frame concern usage. The appropriate concern is specified for the element.

##### Framing a concern via the owned frame concern usage itself as the framed concern

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor1Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, declare the frame concern keyword followed by its name.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

frameconcernbrakingRequirementConcern{ // named framed concern 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue; 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
} 
}

###### Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select a requirement symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the frame concern element.
  2. Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the frames compartment, click the Create frame concern button. The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
2. Specify the concern. The new element is created and displayed in the frames compartment.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **frame concern**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **frame concern**compartment section's bar, click the **Create frame concern**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the concern. The element is created in the frames compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DistanceValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">frame</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">concern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirementConcern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named framed concern</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DistanceValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="2e02e353-4d9b-47fe-a1f1-a27ac0a11e74">Do one of the following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select a requirement symbol and click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>frame concern</strong> element.</li><li>Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the <strong>frames </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create frame concern<span> </span></strong>button.<br />The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="295b213b-3d60-4de7-813c-9b34e268e6a5"><span>Specify the concern.<br /></span>The new element is created and displayed in the<span> </span><strong>frames </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1b6f3ad9-49e6-43a8-a189-7bf1d74fba97"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1c412fbd-3744-4ea1-81ce-139f84f0e95a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>frame concern </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>frame concern </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create frame concern </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7e150abe-0cc1-43b2-8bf7-393182c36618">Specify the concern.<br />The element is created in the <strong>frames </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005420 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: (2026x Refresh1) Functions

- page_id: `304005420`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005420/2026x+Refresh1+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:30.749+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following libraries contain a variety of functions for your modeling needs. The libraries are included with the installation and automatically loaded with each project.

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Libraries & samples' space=''] provide standard SysML v2 libraries containing multiple collections of functions organized into separate namespaces (e.g., BasicFunctions, NumericalFunctions , etc.) that are available for use.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Libraries & samples' space=''] library contains the <DS_Functions>DassaultSystemesFunctions namespace, which provides custom Dassault Systèmes functions that supplement those provided by the Standard Libraries .

See the pages in this section for detailed information and examples of each function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following libraries contain a variety of functions for your modeling needs. The libraries are included with the installation and automatically loaded with each project.</p><ul><li data-uuid="23a7f4f4-d971-4c5d-8ae0-a7151b446318">The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Standard Libraries]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> provide standard SysML v2 libraries containing multiple collections of functions organized into separate namespaces (e.g., <em>BasicFunctions, NumericalFunctions</em>, etc.) that are available for use.</li><li data-uuid="fd9f88ce-6ff3-4499-a674-f274c2278754">The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS SysML Customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> library contains the <em>&lt;DS_Functions&gt;DassaultSystemesFunctions </em>namespace, which<em> </em>provides custom Dassault Systèmes functions that supplement those provided by the <em>Standard Libraries</em>. </li></ul><p>See the pages in this section for detailed information and examples of each function.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004872 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00103: (2026x Refresh1) Guard conditions

- page_id: `304004872`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004872/2026x+Refresh1+Guard+conditions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:50.601+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions > (2026x Refresh1) Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession****asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is *true*.The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Control nodes' space='']*decide* node.

##### Specifying guard conditions for successions

###### Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the *if*keyword followed by the condition. The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.
2. (Optional) Declare the *else*keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to *false*.
3. Click the**Synchronize**button.

// ------------ Full conditional succession

actiontransportPassenger{ 
... 
decide decideNode;firstdecideNode; // specifies the succession source 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

//------------Shorthandconditionalsuccession

actiontransportPassenger{... 
decidedecideNode; // omits the succession sourceas it is identified from the lexically previous action 'decidedecideNode' 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005508 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00104: (2026x Refresh1) Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog

- page_id: `304005508`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005508/2026x+Refresh1+Hiding+style+attributes+from+the+Symbol+Style+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:28:55.282+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Symbol styles > (2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets > (2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Symbol Styles dialog allows you to customize the styles of individual symbols. However, you can customize a style sheet to hide unnecessary style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds.

- If you hide an attribute from the Symbol Style dialog for a specific element kind, ensure the style sheet is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying style sheets' space=''] , not a single view, to affect the style of the specified element symbols in all views.
- Currently, you can hide attributes from the Symbol Style dialog only via the Textual Editor.

###### Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog

To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space=''] in the Textual Editor.
2. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, locate the part usage element that redefines style.
3. Within the body of the redefining element, do the following:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* package.
  2. Declare an attribute usage and apply the InvisibleInDialog metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition . The available attributes depend on the specified style definitions.For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space=''].For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.
4. Synchronize modifications .

partdefNewStyleSheet:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass=SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
part:>>style:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle{ 
privateimportDS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::*; 
#InvisibleInDialogattribute:>>fillColor;// the style attribute *Fill Color* is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

data-uuid="0d942f4a-b95d-45cd-825b-fe53519686d0">Declare an attribute usage and apply the <em>InvisibleInDialog </em>metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition<span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="922934b4-4737-4b7b-a1b2-9b180f221f01"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the specified style definitions. </span>For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span>.</li><li data-uuid="14d743eb-5b86-41e9-86f0-1a15c1a8cbec">For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ae354ce8-f466-4bbe-8b4e-dd7eee6dc55c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Synchronize modifications</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005207 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: (2026x Refresh1) If action

- page_id: `304005207`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005207/2026x+Refresh1+If+action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:18:21.731+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Behavior > (2026x Refresh1) Actions > (2026x Refresh1) Controlling actions > (2026x Refresh1) Structured control actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An if action usage evaluates a condition expression and then performs a then clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *true*, or, optionally, an else clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *false*.

##### Specifying if actions for actions

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the if action.
2. Specify the if action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed if action usage, declare the keyword if .
    2. For a named if action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
3. (Optional) Specify the else if clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else if action usage, declare the keyword else if .
    2. For a named else if action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
4. (Optional) Specify the else clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else action usage, declare the keyword else .
    2. For a named else action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , and specify the element name.
  2. Specify the body for the else clause.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

// unnamed if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
ifthreat.level==high{// unnamed if action usage followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the then clause action usage 
}elseifthreat.level==medium{// unnamed else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the else if action usage 
}else{// unnamed else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the else action usage 
} 
}

// named if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
actionthreatHighifthreat.level==highactionalarm{// named 'threatHigh' if action usage followed by the if condition expression and a named 'alarm' then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the 'alarm' then clause action usage 
}elseactionthreatMediumifthreat.level==mediumactionnotify{// named 'sendNotification' else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and a named 'notify' then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the 'sendNotification' else if action usage 
}elseactionmonitor{// named 'monitor' else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the 'monitor' else action usage 
} 
}

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the view palette

To specify if actions for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the if button to create an if action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the if symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
