<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246530 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00121: (2026x Refresh1) Managing project categories in the local repository

- page_id: `312246530`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246530/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+project+categories+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:33:48.727+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7eef1c-6c85-4c82-b820-b9265d06c858"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><em>Standard Libraries</em><span> </span>category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Managing project categories in the local repository_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The project categories are highlighted in the image. </h6><h4>Creating new project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To create new project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="61d8e72b-9d0d-4b21-aed1-e870466adef5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="60838c58-13cb-4525-9761-d64e0cec68f9">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="6dc30037-0c50-4839-9317-799d2e78f2e5">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> command. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7e35f7fd-be72-4cc7-b89f-c00608959faa">In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To delete project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5a0a383a-0e1e-41cd-b9a2-7c406566b263"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="d2ec254e-df7e-49b9-b52b-5b4adf47ce72">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Delete Category <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete category button.png" /></ac:image></strong> button. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li><li data-uuid="08d8f53f-f461-4cd5-ac1e-5db06f089abf">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Delete Category </strong>command. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="946d2d08-2b5a-4b2d-b750-73b6c12b81e3">In the <strong>Delete Category </strong>dialog, click <strong>Yes </strong>to confirm the deletion.</li></ol><h4>Renaming project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To rename project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fe96a45c-728b-4af1-9e92-9a37c7b3ca8c"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following: <ol><li data-uuid="89d0df79-e1d5-4e87-8b4d-e534217baac3">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="36e28752-59cd-46ac-83c5-0d7c452b10bb">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> command. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cea5f60c-bad1-4d94-a458-1cc60261d57e">In the <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, specify the new name and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddbc4c8-3b0c-4931-804e-bbbae91126b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To move projects to other project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7a065692-e865-404c-a1df-d32a7629d177"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="eca0be86-c920-4901-993c-f460d9853825">Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move to category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="bfedbfd9-e537-4483-9164-eeb13ff9b922">Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> command. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="bae0f61d-5f77-43cd-acfe-860c675844b2">In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Move To Category </strong>dialog, select the needed category and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Managing empty project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To manage empty project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ed4fc3f2-d89a-4655-93c4-a9ec83852219"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="dd856de9-a5e3-4c55-a5ef-8976a0bb1230">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Show empty categories</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show empty categories button.png" /></ac:image> button to enable or disable the display of empty categories. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246508 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00122: (2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository

- page_id: `312246508`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246508/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+project+imports+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:32:35.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project**** is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name.
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. <span>You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project. </span></span></p><ul><li>The <strong>original project<strong> </strong></strong>is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository. <ul><li data-uuid="6d2344a0-8bbf-4b3f-ad6c-da695ec0f405">If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name. </li><li data-uuid="a0cfe82f-74bf-4858-b3cb-8aeca3cef2d3">If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7e3f09e6-0e40-466b-bf11-f21b66c60206"><strong>Subsequent imports of the same project</strong> are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.<ul><li data-uuid="e8b0764b-3f97-4d0a-9eb4-4fd3f9ac1221">Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23eef6fe-aa62-4b4e-a7ac-c2375bd7d749"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="508ee9cc-674c-43e2-bb5d-dc300f89e591"><ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Renaming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li><li data-uuid="f2e7ae5e-faba-49e9-af65-4ed168ea7f01"><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Removing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.</li><li data-uuid="e04f2e61-e594-48d4-8c70-b394c1d5061d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project categories in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Moving]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can view the project details in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_instances_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246486 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00123: (2026x Refresh1) Managing projects in the local repository

- page_id: `312246486`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246486/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+projects+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:30:27.707+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for creating, opening, importing, removing, renaming, saving, and exporting local projects.

###### Creating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed projects.

###### Importing projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- Open the **Manage Projects**dialog . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository

Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository

Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. Select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

###### Saving projects in the local repository

To save a local project in the local repository

Do one of the following:

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button.

Use the **Change Sets** tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

Export projects from the local repository as *.mdszip* files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens a question dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">See the procedures on this page for creating, opening, importing, removing, renaming, saving, and exporting local projects.</p><h4>Creating projects in the local repository</h4><p>Create projects in the local repository to create your models.</p><p>To create projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee4dc1bd-426f-478b-a2ba-880dc831475d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="49fdd9ba-f50c-4182-95ad-04282f6175c1">In the dialog, click the <strong>New</strong> button. In the <strong>Open</strong> dialog, select the project and click<strong> Open</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="c815147d-bdf5-4ffb-8442-65f72ea16198">In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Open Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="2c085453-ce82-47b6-8a1c-e605bb68abe5">On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+O</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="43ef0d7e-c5ca-4213-bb82-09c2fcea84d3">Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.</li></ul><h4>Removing projects from the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cca7795f-56af-47dc-b371-cc6e5bd6c2b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. The question dialog opens. Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button to confirm the removal of the project.</li></ol><h4>Renaming projects in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b73cec7-d808-487d-b907-a8dd4a7138e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1da4df71-dafa-4d67-816c-e5751622bae1">rename</ac:inline-comment-marker> projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="91711a88-b145-4af2-a80d-06c7e431dc22"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="396c4e95-1bde-405f-9c9b-dc2cffd6e82e">Select the needed project and click the <strong>Rename</strong> button. The <strong>Rename </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="d58279fe-fb67-4fab-a373-00566291e6d9">In the dialog, update the project name and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Saving projects in the local repository</h4><p>To save a local project in the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S. </li><li>In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Save Project </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1074d31-938f-43d9-82a5-4710cb6ca8d9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the <strong>Change Sets</strong> tab to track project modifications. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="d6ed4058-2971-482f-b19d-3a8933ba1194">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="0b07e60e-1b1e-466b-974d-d2ac78e97243">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change Sets.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Exporting projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</h4><p>Export projects from the local repository as <em>.mdszip</em> files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.</p><p>To export projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5cf7e9a1-da44-47f9-9912-66fd996ddc36">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project As</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3afcd461-f40b-4799-bb11-1109833648f2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the <strong>Save As Project</strong> command opens a question dialog. Click <strong>Save</strong> in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="7b98258a-32b0-4d80-8c0e-568e2ec647e6">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004980 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00124: (2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells

- page_id: `304004980`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004980/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+tabular+view+cells
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:58.799+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space=''] cells are the intersections of every row and column, representing the appropriate element values. Tabular view cells may contain singular or multiple values.

You can modify a table cell either directly through the cell or via the **Review and Modify**dialog; however, the availability of these functionalities depends on the feature displayed by the cell's column. For more information, see the notes in and subsections.

Tabular view cells are modeled via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Modifying a table cell directly via the cell

You can modify a table cell directly via the cell if it is a*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular view columns' space='']* column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values. E.g., declared name, ID, etc. It doesn't apply to cells listed in the note for the subsection.

###### Modifying a table cell value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify.
2. Specify the new value.
3. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.

###### Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify and do one of the following:
  1. Input the new value.
  2. Click the value field to display the dropdown menu with suggested applicable multiplicity values.
2. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes. [ATTACHMENT filename='Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell.png']

##### Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog

You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

You can modify a table cell via the**Review and Modify**dialog if it is:

- Element-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - SysML::Definition::owned<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Definition::ownedAction
  - SysML::Usage::nested<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Usage::nestedAction
  - KerML::Element::ownedElement
  - KerML::Type::ownedFeature
  - KerML::Namespace::ownedMember
- Multi-primitive-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - KerML::Element::aliasIds (multi-string)
  - KerML::Element::documentation (multi-string) - allows you to edit the owned documentation body's text, not the documentation element itself. For example, creating a new string creates a new documentation element for the rowElement with the specified documentation body.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Review and Modify dialog

To open the Review and Modify dialog

Do one of the following:

- Double-click the cell or an element in the cell. The More button appears. Click the button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 1.png']
- In a tabular view, click the +<number> more button at the bottom of the cell. Applicable only for multi-valued cells. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 2.png'] The Review and Modify dialog opens, displaying all values of the cell.

###### Creating new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To create new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the Add New [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog Add New button.png'] button. The Create Element dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, click the needed element. The element is created in the cell at the bottom of the list.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Deleting elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To delete elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements.
  1. (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog select element.png']
3. Click the Delete [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Renaming elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To rename elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the row of the element and rename as needed.
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

##### Managing multi-valued cells

Tabular view cells may contain multiple values. By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

###### Filtering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To filter elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. At the top of the dialog, input the filter keyword. The filter updates as you type.
  1. To reset the filter, click the Clear [IMAGE alt='' src='']**** button.
3. Click OK or Cancel .

###### Reordering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To reorder elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to move and click the Move [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon. Drag and drop the row element to the needed position. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog move element.png']
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Modifying the value count in multi-valued cells

By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can modify the limit via the *maxDisplayedValues* attribute specified for the *DS_Views::CoreViews::Base Table*, which is reused by all predefined tabular views.

To modify the value count in multi-valued cells

1. Within the body of a view, declare an attribute redefining the maxDisplayedValues attribute and set the default limit.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
attribute:>>maxDisplayedValuesdefault3;// the default value limit is set to 3 for the table's multi-value cells 
renderrendering:>>asTable{...} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tabular view cell errors

You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:

| Icon / Button | Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Click to load the cell. | Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the Refresh button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. |
|  | Failed to evaluate expression. | Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element. |
|  | Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells. | Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.To resolve the issue, see the Tabular view loading warning box in the subsection on the page. |

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="maxDisplayedValues attribute.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Tabular view cell errors</h3><p>You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon / Button</th><th scope="col">Message</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click to load the cell icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to load the cell.</td><td><p>Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.</p><p>If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. </p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Failed to evaluate expression icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Failed to evaluate expression.</td><td>Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells.</td><td><p>Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To resolve the issue, see the <strong>Tabular view loading </strong>warning box in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filtering tabular view column cells]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> subsection on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304005008 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00125: (2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view rows

- page_id: `304005008`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005008/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+tabular+view+rows
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:21:43.803+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Views > (2026x Refresh1) Creating views > (2026x Refresh1) Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each table row appearing along the side of the table represents a model element. The procedures on this page describe how to manage elements in tabular views, such as how to add, remove, delete them, etc.

##### Adding elements to the table

###### Adding elements to the table via drag and drop

Add already created elements to the table.

To add elements to the table via drag and drop

- S elect element(s) in the Containment tree or element symbol(s) in a view, then drag and drop them onto the diagrammatic representation of a tabular view. 
The element is added to the table. The element position may change after reopening or reloading the table, depending on the row sorting.If the dropped elements do not comply with the filter conditions specified for the table or the elements are already added to the table, the elements are not added to the table, and the following notification is displayed.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding elements to the table via the Add New command

Create a new element and add it to the table.

To add elements to the table via the Add New command

- In the tabular view's toolbar, click the Add New button, and in the dropdown menu, click the element you want to create and add to the table. The element is created in the model and displayed in the table by exposing it.

- The Add New dropdown menu complies with the filters specified for the view. If certain elements are filtered out for the view, they do not appear in the dropdown menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='Table Row Management Add New Button.png']
- By default, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] that is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''] is used to suggest elements to be created in the Add New dropdown menu. If you want a custom table-specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) Add New dropdown menu instead, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu' space=''] page.

###### Hiding elements from the table

Hiding elements from the table filters out the selected elements from the table view without permanently .

###### Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

To hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to hide, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Remove From Table button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Remove From Table . [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 3.png'] The row element is hidden from the table.

##### Deleting elements from the table

###### Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command

To delete elements from the table via the Delete command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Delete button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Delete . [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 3.png'] The row element is deleted from the table and the model.

##### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree

###### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

To select table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Select in Containment Tree command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, the row element is selected in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to select the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 2.png']

##### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements

###### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

To open the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Specification command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, you can open the Specification Panel for the row element (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to open the Specification Panel for the row element or the cell value (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 2.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004737 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00126: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Textual Editor

- page_id: `304004737`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004737/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Textual+Editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:43.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic procedures for working with the Textual Editor and the textual notation.

For SysML v2 Textual Editor-related environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] page.

##### Managing the Textual Editor

###### Opening the Textual Editor

To open the Textual Editor

- In the Containment tree, right-click an element. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- On a view , right-click an element symbol. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tabular views' space=''] , click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In a tabular view, right-click a column header. In the shortcut menu, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space='']'s Navigation bar, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''], click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to a compartment element or the element specified in the **Type** field and, in its submenu, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification panel' space=''], click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Result Expression**field.

###### Saving changes in the Textual Editor

To save changes in the Textual Editor

Do one of the following:

- In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the Synchronize button [ATTACHMENT filename='sync_button.png'] .
- Use the Alt+S shortcut keys. You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor. If the **Synchronize model with errors** environment option is set to:*true*(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.*false*, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.

###### Updating changes in the Textual EditorUpdating changes in the Textual Editor

Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is open, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, there are two options:

- Click the **Refresh** button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.
- Click the **Synchronize** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor. You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the **Refresh**button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting in the Textual Editor

- There can be two types of comments:
  - A line note.
    - This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a comment element in your model.
    - Two ways to write it:
      - Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.
      - Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.
  - A comment element.
    - This type of comment creates a comment element in your model.
    - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Annotations' space=''] page.
- By default, comments are indicated in #248000 color, differentiating them from other notational text. // May require revision - line note; a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor. 
// the following two options are equivalent; both create a *comment*element with the 'May require revision' text: 
/* May require revision */ 
comment/* May require revision */

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Textual Editor</h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To save changes in the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the <strong>Synchronize</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Use the Alt+S shortcut keys.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b42913d8-f543-4f10-9714-2b153936282c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31055868-951f-455e-8f4b-5708ab064c2d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Synchronize model with errors</strong> environment option is set to:</p><ul><li><em>true </em>(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.</li><li><em>false</em>, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f536a4a9-4efd-4978-9d8b-60f50c40df2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Updating changes in the Textual Editor</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Updating changes in the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb2491d2-77c3-4698-9763-79f22cae5b7c">open</ac:inline-comment-marker>, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.<br /></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="outdated_textual_notation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In this case, there are two options:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong></span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_button.png" /></ac:image> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="642d8e21-4e29-40ca-82ea-4c2e305715c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Refresh</strong> </span>button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_IDs_in_textual_notation.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4>Commenting in the Textual Editor</h4><ul><li>There can be two types of comments:<ul><li>A line note. <ul><li>This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a <em>comment </em>element in your model.</li><li>Two ways to write it:<ul><li>Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.</li><li>Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>A comment element. <ul><li>This type of comment creates a <em>comment </em>element in your model.</li><li>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Annotations" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">By default, comments are indicated in <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-lime,#4c6b1f);">#248000</span> color, differentiating them from other notational text.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="137005f8-340b-44e5-b4fe-4e630c56f533"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// May require revision - line note; a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor.<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following two options are equivalent; both create a <em>comment </em>element with the 'May require revision' text:<br /></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">comment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* May require revision */</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006219 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: (2026x Refresh1) Managing textual notation

- page_id: `304006219`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006219/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+textual+notation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:12.502+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The sections on this page provide the basic information for writing the textual notation usingthe [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor' space=''].

##### Element declaration

- An element is declared with (1) a keyword indicating the element kind, followed by (2) the element name, and then either (3) curly braces for specifying the element's body or a semicolon to indicate the end of the element's specification. part flashlight;// keyword 'part', followed by the element name, and a semicolon, indicating the end of the element specification. 
part 'LED Flashlight' {// opening curly braces for specifying the declared element's body 
 part bulb;// element's body 
}// closing curly braces indicating the end of the element specification. 
part; // declared element without a name

##### Keywords

- Keywords indicate element kind ( part def, part, flow, item , etc.), relationships ( subsets, specializes, redefines ), or additional properties, such as directions ( in, out, to, from , etc.).
- Keywords are always typed in lowercase.
- Special textual symbols may replace specialization keywords. See the subpages under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] page.
- By default, keywords are indicated in #1D0FFF color, differentiating them from other notational text.
- Multiple keywords may be specified in a defined order: (1) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifiers' space=''] (direction, derived , abstract , constant , ref ), (2) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Occurrences' space=''] , (3) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Occurrences' space=''] (snapshot, timeslice), (4) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metadata' space=''] , (5) element metaclass, (6) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element names' space=''] , (7) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Multiplicity' space=''] , (8) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specializations' space=''] . individualtimeslicepartmyCardefinedbyCar;// individual, portion kind (timeslice), element metaclass (part), element name (myCar), specialization (defined by Car).
- For all element keyword examples, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model composition' space=''] section.

##### Naming

- Unrestricted element names that include whitespace, special characters, or that correspond to keywords, must be encapsulated with single quotation marks.
  - Press the quotation mark (or Shift+quotation mark) key to encapsulate a text selection with single or double quotation marks.
- Basic element names without special characters or whitespace do not have to be encapsulated. 
For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element names' space=''] page. part 'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; //basic element name without whitespace 
}

##### Name resolution

For name resolution-specific environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] page.

Name resolution allows you to target an element by its name, which can be done in the following ways:

- Feature chaining
  - A feature chain allows you to target a specific usage element located within the scope of another usage element, as long as the targeting and the target elements are within the scope of the same parent element.
  - A feature chain can only chain usage elements.
  - A feature chain is specified as a sequence of two or more usage element names separated by dot symbols. [ATTACHMENT filename='feature_chaining.png']
- Qualified name
  - A qualified name identifies an element qualified by the namespace relative to which the element name is to be resolved.
  - A qualified name can be used to target any element (definitions, usages, packages).
  - A qualified name is specified as a sequence of segments that indicate namespaces, which are separated by the :: symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='qualified_name.png']

##### Textual notation errors

For textual notation error management, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space=''] page.

If a portion of the textual notation is incompatible or cannot be rendered, error messages may appear in place of the invalid content. Although rare, this may occur when importing a non-compliant model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006299 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: (2026x Refresh1) Metadata

- page_id: `304006299`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304006299/2026x+Refresh1+Metadata
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:19:22.153+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Model composition > (2026x Refresh1) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Metadata allows extending model elements by adding various attributes, semantics, and notation. It can be used to customize various models for specific domains or purposes.Metadata can be applied to any element. Metadata usage annotates its containing element; otherwise, an annotation relationship can be specified using the *about*syntax.

#### INFO: Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic metadata refers to a metadata definition element that specializes the *SemanticMetadata* metaclass (e.g., *<moe> MeasureOfEffectiveness*, *<derive> DerivedRequirementMetadata*, etc.). There are several key differences in how elements are displayed in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances, depending on the kind of metadata applied to them:

If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:

- The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., part ) is not displayed.
- The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The def keyword is appended for definition elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='semantic_metadata.png']

If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:

- The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, the element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. [ATTACHMENT filename='non-semantic_metadata.png']

##### Creating metadata definition elements

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the metadata def keyword .
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes in the element body.
4. Click the **Synchronize**button.

metadata defApproval{attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
}

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

To create metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the metadata def element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the metadata attributes.

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the view palette

To create metadata definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Parts/Attributes group, click the **metadata** def button to create a metadata definition element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes.

##### Applying metadata to an element

###### Applying metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

To apply metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element for which you want to apply metadata, do one of the following:
  1. For unnamed metadata usage, do one of the following:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata followed by the metadata definition name. The notational difference between using the textual symbol @ or the keyword *metadata* depends on the **Display Metadata as Keywords** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Environment options' space='']. By default, it is set to *false*, thus the notational representation of metadata in the textual notation is set to the textual symbol @. Even if you use a different notation, once the Textual Editor is reopened, the notational representation is changed to the one corresponding to the environment option. Set the environment option to *true* to use the keyword *metadata*notational representation instead.
  2. For a named metadata usage element:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata, specify the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
2. Specify the metadata attribute values in the element body.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. // unnamed metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@Approval{ // the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name (step 1.a.i)approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}/* or */ 
metadataApproval{ // the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata definition name (step 1.a.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
 } 
}// named metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@approvalSystem:Approval{ ****// the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata usage name, defined by the metadata definition (step 1.a.i) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}metadataapprovalSystem:Approval{// the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata usage name, defined by metadata definition (step 1.b.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
} 
}

###### Applying metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

Using the following notation applies the metadata to an element without the possibility of specifying the metadata attributes.

To apply metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, prior to the element keyword of the element you want to apply the metadata to, type the hash sign # followed by the metadata definition element name.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. #Approvalpartsystem;

###### Applying metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To apply metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view using

- Drag and drop a metadata element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Apply Metadata command.

###### Applying annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

For other element annotation options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Annotations' space=''] page.

To apply annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

Whether you want to apply annotation metadata with or without attributes, the overall procedure is generally the same as for:

- 

However, in this case:

- The element to which the annotation metadata is applied must be declared separately.
- The applied metadata keyword is followed by the keyword about and the name of the element to which the annotation metadata is to be applied.

partsystem;// element declaration

@Approvalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

/* or */

metadataApprovalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Metadata allows extending model elements by adding various attributes, semantics, and notation. It can be used to customize various models for specific domains or purposes. </span>Metadata can be applied to any element. Metadata usage annotates its containing element; otherwise, an annotation relationship can be specified using the <em>about </em>syntax.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de96df3d-418f-45d3-829e-6d3410c47ed8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Semantic metadata refers to a metadata definition element that specializes the <em>SemanticMetadata</em> metaclass (e.g., <em>&lt;moe&gt; MeasureOfEffectiveness</em>, <em>&lt;derive&gt; DerivedRequirementMetadata</em>, etc.). There are several key differences in how elements are displayed in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances, depending on the kind of metadata applied to them:</p><p>If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2e1486f6-00db-42ed-b5f0-23796941864b">The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is not displayed.</li><li data-uuid="f728d90b-2ca2-4c7d-a87e-414d6176930c">The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The <em>def</em> keyword is appended for definition elements. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="semantic_metadata.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p>If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:</p><ul><li data-uuid="302b4ae8-124e-422b-a39b-ba5606388108">The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, the element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="non-semantic_metadata.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating metadata definition elements</h3><h4>Creating metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create metadata definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>metadata def </em>keyword<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the metadata attributes in the element body.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>metad<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="86d4f0de-0443-4c67-ba61-6a54bf328e39">ata def</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Approval</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approved</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating metadata definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create metadata definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>metadata def</strong> element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the metadata attributes.</li></ol><h4>Creating metadata definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create metadata definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Items/Parts/Attributes group, click the<span> <strong>metadata </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create a metadata definition element.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the element.</li><li>Specify the metadata attributes.</li></ol><h3><span>Applying metadata to an element</span> </h3><h4>Applying metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To apply metadata with attributes to an element via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element for which you want to apply metadata, do one of the following:</p><ol><li>For unnamed metadata usage, do one of the following:<ol><li>Declare the <em>@</em> symbol followed by the metadata definition element name.</li><li>Declare the keyword <em>metadata</em><em> </em>followed by the metadata definition name.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad541e78-feb8-4e50-bcce-919eadaf772a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The notational difference between using the textual symbol @ or the keyword <em>metadata</em> depends on the <strong>Display Metadata as Keywords</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[environment option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. By default, it is set to <em>false</em>, thus the notational representation of metadata in the textual notation is set to the textual symbol @. Even if you use a different notation, once the Textual Editor is reopened, the notational representation is changed to the one corresponding to the environment option. Set the environment option to <em>true</em> to use the keyword <em>metadata </em>notational representation instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For a named metadata usage element:</p><ol><li>Declare the <em>@</em> symbol followed by the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.</li><li>Declare the keyword <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="776993b9-3949-4cd2-9feb-3358f65ab9d9">metadata, </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="776993b9-3949-4cd2-9feb-3358f65ab9d9">specify the element name, and then define </ac:inline-comment-marker>it by the metadata definition.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>Specify the metadata attribute values in the element body.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94b2a80c-81dd-4880-80ad-76f2fd16628b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed metadata usage defined by metadata definition:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span
````
