## PAGE 00131: (2026x Refresh1) Model customization

- page_id: `304005193`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304005193/2026x+Refresh1+Model+customization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:24:27.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model customization allows you to customize certain predefined model components based on your needs and preferences. You can create model-based custom views with customized table columns or view palettes, customize symbolic view symbol styles, view creation dialogs, and validation suites with custom validation rules. Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined components, you can model them yourself based on your project-specific needs.

The following model components are available for model-based customization.

###### Views customization

Model-based views customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model composition' space=''] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Views' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tabular view definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom tabular view definitions' space='']

###### Symbol styles customization

Model-based symbol styles customization is described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model composition' space=''] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Views' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom style sheets' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extracting style sheets' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating style sheets' space='']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a style sheet condition' space='']
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating rules for a style sheet' space='']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying a condition for a rule' space='']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying styles for a rule' space='']
        - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating reusable styles' space='']
        - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog' space='']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Style sheet styles tables' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space='']

###### Validation suites and rules customization

Model-based validation suites and rules customization are described under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space=''] section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom validation suites and rules' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom validation suites and rules' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating validation suites and rules' space='']

###### Creation dialogs customization

Model-based creation dialogs customization is described under the section's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs' space=''] subsection:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom creation dialogs' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom View Creation dialog' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom Element Creation dialog' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom Relationship Creation dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004596 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: (2026x Refresh1) Model management

- page_id: `304004596`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004596/2026x+Refresh1+Model+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:39.684+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Model management options

###### Containment tree

The Containment tree displays the hierarchy of your model elements, allowing you to review, organize, and modify the model.By default, the Containment tree has the Essential Elements Only mode enabled, displaying the model's essential elements most relevant to your modeling process. Disabling this option displays all model elements, including those less relevant to your modeling process. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Containment tree' space=''] page.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Textual Editor

Textual Editor is akin to a code editor allowing you to view and create your model using textual notation in a code-like format. The Textual Editor displays the model of a specific root namespace. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Textual Editor' space=''] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Views

Views allow you to create a specific view of the model and create and modify model elements and their symbols. They do not display the model in its entirety with all of the elements the model contains. A view represents a particular set of model elements, encompassing a particular view of the model. 
Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with views' space=''] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004780 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch

- page_id: `304004780`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004780/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Patch
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:45.893+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model Patch technology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.

You can use the Model Patch mechanism:

- As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.
- For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.
- To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.

To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see the subpages in this section.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="309936d7-3604-489b-9b77-a054dd20a079">Model Patch</ac:inline-comment-marker> technology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.</p><p>You can use the Model Patch mechanism:</p><ul><li>As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.</li><li>For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.</li><li>To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.</li></ul><p>To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see the subpages in this section.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004782 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types

- page_id: `304004782`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304004782/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Patch+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:17:46.141+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Project management > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a model patch, you have two options to choose from: **All selected content**and **Changes from selected version to version #X**. This topic explains the difference between the two options and when to use each one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a model patch type.

##### All selected content

Model patches of the *All selected content* type can be created from and applied to local, Teamwork Cloud, and **3D**EXPERIENCE platform projects.

Using this patch type, you can bring all the selected elements as they are. This patch type is always considered to be the source of truth, meaning that the patch content overrides your existing elements and/or adds elements that exist in the source version but not in the target.

Let's say that your source model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements, and your target model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements. If you decide to apply a model patch of the **All selected content** type containing the *Requirements* package to the target model, the *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements are added to the target model, while the *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements are deleted. In other words, the whole *Requirements* package in the target model is overwritten with data from the patch.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *All selected content* type.

##### Changes from selected version to version #X

You can only create a model patch of the **Changes from selected version to version #X** type from a Teamwork Cloud project. However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.

If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, choose to bring changes from the selected version when creating a patch

Let's say that your source model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements, and your target model contains the *Requirements* package with *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements. If you apply a model patch of the **Changes from selected version to version #X** type containing the *Requirements* package, the *maxFuelFlowSpeed* and *minFuelFlowSpeed* requirements are added from the patch while the *maxFuelCapacity* and *minFuelCapacity* requirements are retained the target model. In other words the target model data is not overwritten but complemented with data from the patch.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *Changes from selected version to version #X* type.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In other words, the whole <em>Requirements</em> package in the target model is overwritten with data from the patch.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_all_selected_content.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>All selected content</em> type.</h6><h3>Changes from selected version to version #X</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="281f347a-3092-4dc4-ac62-ff660a118565"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only create a model patch of the <strong>Changes from selected version to version #X</strong> type from a Teamwork Cloud project. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, choose to bring changes from the selected version when creating a patch</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Let's say that your source model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements, and your target model contains the <em>Requirements</em> package with <em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements. If you apply a model patch of the <strong>Changes from selected version to version #X</strong> type containing the <em>Requirements</em> package, the <em>maxFuelFlowSpeed</em> and <em>minFuelFlowSpeed</em> requirements are added from the patch while the <em>maxFuelCapacity</em> and <em>minFuelCapacity</em> requirements are retained the target model. In other words the target model data is not overwritten but complemented with data from the patch.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_changes_form_specific_version.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>Changes from selected version to version #X</em> type.</h6>
````
